name: Bhagavad Gita
source: Transliterations: krishnagita npm package (public domain). Translations: Swami Sivananda public domain edition.

verses.transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca / dharmakṣetre kurukṣetre samavetā yuyutsavaḥ / māmakāḥ pāṇḍavāś caiva kim akurvata saṃjaya /
verses.translation: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjaya, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do when they assembled at Kurukshetra, eager to fight?

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / dṛṣṭvā tu pāṇḍavānīkaṃ vyūḍhaṃ duryodhanas tadā / ācāryam upasaṃgamya rājā vacanam abravīt /
verses.translation: Sanjaya said: Having seen the army of the Pandavas arrayed in battle order, King Duryodhana then approached his teacher Dronacharya and spoke these words.

verses.transliteration: paśyaitāṃ pāṇḍuputrāṇām ācārya mahatīṃ camūm / vyūḍhāṃ drupadaputreṇa tava śiṣyeṇa dhīmatā /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 3: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: atra śūrā maheṣvāsā bhīmārjunasamā yudhi / yuyudhāno virāṭaś ca drupadaś ca mahārathaḥ /
verses.translation: Here are heroic bowmen equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna — Yuyudhana, Virata, and Drupada, the great warrior.

verses.transliteration: dhṛṣṭaketuś cekitānaḥ kāśirājaś ca vīryavān / purujit kuntibhojaś ca śaibyaś ca narapuṃgavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 5: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yudhāmanyuś ca vikrānta uttamaujāś ca vīryavān / saubhadro draupadeyāś ca sarva eva mahārathāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 6: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: asmākaṃ tu viśiṣṭā ye tān nibodha dvijottama / nāyakā mama sainyasya saṃjñārthaṃ tān bravīmi te /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 7: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhavān bhīṣmaś ca karṇaś ca kṛpaś ca samitiṃjayaḥ / aśvatthāmā vikarṇaś ca saumadattir jayadrathaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 8: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anye ca bahavaḥ śūrā madarthe tyaktajīvitāḥ / nānāśastrapraharaṇāḥ sarve yuddhaviśāradāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 9: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aparyāptaṃ tad asmākaṃ balaṃ bhīṣmābhirakṣitam / paryāptaṃ tv idam eteṣāṃ balaṃ bhīmābhirakṣitam /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 10: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ayaneṣu ca sarveṣu yathābhāgam avasthitāḥ / bhīṣmam evābhirakṣantu bhavantaḥ sarva eva hi /
verses.translation: All of you, stationed in your respective positions in the divisions of the army, protect Bhishma in particular.

verses.transliteration: tasya saṃjanayan harṣaṃ kuruvṛddhaḥ pitāmahaḥ / siṃhanādaṃ vinadyoccaiḥ śaṅkhaṃ dadhmau pratāpavān /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 12: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhāś ca bheryaś ca paṇavānakagomukhāḥ / sahasaivābhyahanyanta sa śabdas tumulo 'bhavat /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 13: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tataḥ śvetair hayair yukte mahati syandane sthitau / mādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaś caiva divyau śaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 14: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pāñcajanyaṃ hṛṣīkeśo devadattaṃ dhanaṃjayaḥ / pauṇḍraṃ dadhmau mahāśaṅkhaṃ bhīmakarmā vṛkodaraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 15: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anantavijayaṃ rājā kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ / nakulaḥ sahadevaś ca sughoṣamaṇipuṣpakau /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 16: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāśyaś ca parameṣvāsaḥ śikhaṇḍī ca mahārathaḥ / dhṛṣṭadyumno virāṭaś ca sātyakiś cāparājitaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 17: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: drupado draupadeyāś ca sarvaśaḥ pṛthivīpate / saubhadraś ca mahābāhuḥ śaṅkhān dadhmuḥ pṛthak pṛthak /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 18: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sa ghoṣo dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṃ hṛdayāni vyadārayat / nabhaś ca pṛthivīṃ caiva tumulo vyanunādayan /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 19: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: atha vyavasthitān dṛṣṭvā dhārtarāṣṭrān kapidhvajaḥ / pravṛtte śastrasaṃpāte dhanur udyamya pāṇḍavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 20: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: hṛṣīkeśaṃ tadā vākyam idam āha mahīpate / senayor ubhayor madhye rathaṃ sthāpaya me 'cyuta /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 21: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yāvad etān nirīkṣe 'haṃ yoddhukāmān avasthitān / kair mayā saha yoddhavyam asmin raṇasamudyame /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 22: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yotsyamānān avekṣe 'haṃ ya ete 'tra samāgatāḥ / dhārtarāṣṭrasya durbuddher yuddhe priyacikīrṣavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 23: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: evam ukto hṛṣīkeśo guḍākeśena bhārata / senayor ubhayor madhye sthāpayitvā rathottamam /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 24: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇapramukhataḥ sarveṣāṃ ca mahīkṣitām / uvāca pārtha paśyaitān samavetān kurūn iti /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 25: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tatrāpaśyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝn atha pitāmahān / ācāryān mātulān bhrātṝn putrān pautrān sakhīṃs tathā /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 26: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śvaśurān suhṛdaś caiva senayor ubhayor api / tān samīkṣya sa kaunteyaḥ sarvān bandhūn avasthitān /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 27: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kṛpayā parayāviṣṭo viṣīdann idam abravīt / dṛṣṭvemaṃ svajanaṃ kṛṣṇa yuyutsuṃ samupasthitam /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: O Krishna, seeing my kinsmen arrayed here, eager to fight, my limbs fail and my mouth is parched.

verses.transliteration: sīdanti mama gātrāṇi mukhaṃ ca pariśuṣyati / vepathuś ca śarīre me romaharṣaś ca jāyate /
verses.translation: My body quivers and my hair stands on end; my bow slips from my hand and my skin burns all over.

verses.transliteration: gāṇḍīvaṃ sraṃsate hastāt tvak caiva paridahyate / na ca śaknomy avasthātuṃ bhramatīva ca me manaḥ /
verses.translation: I am unable to stand; my mind seems to whirl; I see adverse omens, O Keshava.

verses.transliteration: nimittāni ca paśyāmi viparītāni keśava / na ca śreyo 'nupaśyāmi hatvā svajanam āhave /
verses.translation: Nor do I see any good in killing my kinsmen in battle. O Krishna, I desire neither victory, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.

verses.transliteration: na kāṅkṣe vijayaṃ kṛṣṇa na ca rājyaṃ sukhāni ca / kiṃ no rājyena govinda kiṃ bhogair jīvitena vā /
verses.translation: Of what use is kingdom, pleasures, or even life, O Govinda, when all those for whom we desire them stand here ready to battle?

verses.transliteration: yeṣām arthe kāṅkṣitaṃ no rājyaṃ bhogāḥ sukhāni ca / ta ime 'vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṃs tyaktvā dhanāni ca /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 33: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ācāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva ca pitāmahāḥ / mātulāḥ śvaśurāḥ pautrāḥ śyālāḥ saṃbandhinas tathā /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 34: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: etān na hantum icchāmi ghnato 'pi madhusūdana / api trailokyarājyasya hetoḥ kiṃ nu mahīkṛte /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 35: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nihatya dhārtarāṣṭrān naḥ kā prītiḥ syāj janārdana / pāpam evāśrayed asmān hatvaitān ātatāyinaḥ /
verses.translation: Sin will only accrue to us if we slay these aggressors. Therefore, we should not kill the sons of Dhritarashtra.

verses.transliteration: tasmān nārhā vayaṃ hantuṃ dhārtarāṣṭrān svabāndhavān / svajanaṃ hi kathaṃ hatvā sukhinaḥ syāma mādhava /
verses.translation: Although these men, overtaken by greed, see no fault in destroying one's own family, why should we, who can clearly see the crime of such destruction, engage in this sinful act?

verses.transliteration: yady apy ete na paśyanti lobhopahatacetasaḥ / kulakṣayakṛtaṃ doṣaṃ mitradrohe ca pātakam /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 38: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kathaṃ na jñeyam asmābhiḥ pāpād asmān nivartitum / kulakṣayakṛtaṃ doṣaṃ prapaśyadbhir janārdana /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 39: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kulakṣaye praṇaśyanti kuladharmāḥ sanātanāḥ / dharme naṣṭe kulaṃ kṛtsnam adharmo 'bhibhavaty uta /
verses.translation: When the family is destroyed, the eternal family traditions are lost, and the rest of the family becomes involved in irreligion.

verses.transliteration: adharmābhibhavāt kṛṣṇa praduṣyanti kulastriyaḥ / strīṣu duṣṭāsu vārṣṇeya jāyate varṇasaṃkaraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 41: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃkaro narakāyaiva kulaghnānāṃ kulasya ca / patanti pitaro hy eṣāṃ luptapiṇḍodakakriyāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 42: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: doṣair etaiḥ kulaghnānāṃ varṇasaṃkarakārakaiḥ / utsādyante jātidharmāḥ kuladharmāś ca śāśvatāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 43: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: utsannakuladharmāṇāṃ manuṣyāṇāṃ janārdana / narake niyataṃ vāso bhavatīty anuśuśruma /
verses.translation: In the Arjuna Vishada Yoga (Chapter 1), verse 44: Arjuna sees his kinsmen on the battlefield and is overcome with grief and moral confusion. He lays down his bow, unable to fight. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aho bata mahat pāpaṃ kartuṃ vyavasitā vayam / yad rājyasukhalobhena hantuṃ svajanam udyatāḥ /
verses.translation: Oh, how strange and terrible it is that we are engaged in committing great sins, driven by the desire to enjoy royal happiness.

verses.transliteration: yadi mām apratīkāram aśastraṃ śastrapāṇayaḥ / dhārtarāṣṭrā raṇe hanyus tan me kṣemataraṃ bhavet /
verses.translation: It would be better for me if the sons of Dhritarashtra, weapons in hand, were to kill me unarmed and unresisting on the battlefield.

verses.transliteration: evam uktvārjunaḥ saṃkhye rathopastha upāviśat / visṛjya saśaraṃ cāpaṃ śokasaṃvignamānasaḥ /
verses.translation: Sanjaya said: Having thus spoken, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot in the midst of the battlefield, his mind overwhelmed with grief.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / taṃ tathā kṛpayāviṣṭam aśrupūrṇākulekṣaṇam / viṣīdantam idaṃ vākyam uvāca madhusūdanaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 1: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / kutas tvā kaśmalam idaṃ viṣame samupasthitam / anāryajuṣṭam asvargyam akīrtikaram arjuna /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Whence has come upon you, O Arjuna, in this crisis, this despondency, this unmanliness? Do not yield to impotence, O Arjuna. It does not befit you. Shake off your fainthearted­ness and arise.

verses.transliteration: klaibyaṃ mā sma gamaḥ pārtha naitat tvayy upapadyate / kṣudraṃ hṛdayadaurbalyaṃ tyaktvottiṣṭha paraṃtapa /
verses.translation: O Arjuna, do not yield to unmanliness. This does not befit you. Shake off your faint-heartedness and arise.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / kathaṃ bhīṣmam ahaṃ saṃkhye droṇaṃ ca madhusūdana / iṣubhiḥ pratiyotsyāmi pūjārhāv arisūdana /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 4: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: gurūn ahatvā hi mahānubhāvāñ; śreyo bhoktuṃ bhaikṣyam apīha loke / hatvārthakāmāṃs tu gurūn ihaiva; bhuñjīya bhogān rudhirapradigdhān /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 5: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na caitad vidmaḥ kataran no garīyo; yad vā jayema yadi vā no jayeyuḥ / yān eva hatvā na jijīviṣāmas; te 'vasthitāḥ pramukhe dhārtarāṣṭrāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 6: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kārpaṇyadoṣopahatasvabhāvaḥ; pṛcchāmi tvāṃ dharmasaṃmūḍhacetāḥ / yac chreyaḥ syān niścitaṃ brūhi tan me; śiṣyas te 'haṃ śādhi māṃ tvāṃ prapannam /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 7: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na hi prapaśyāmi mamāpanudyād; yac chokam ucchoṣaṇam indriyāṇām / avāpya bhūmāv asapatnam ṛddhaṃ; rājyaṃ surāṇām api cādhipatyam /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 8: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / evam uktvā hṛṣīkeśaṃ guḍākeśaḥ paraṃtapaḥ / na yotsya iti govindam uktvā tūṣṇīṃ babhūva ha /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 9: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tam uvāca hṛṣīkeśaḥ prahasann iva bhārata / senayor ubhayor madhye viṣīdantam idaṃ vacaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 10: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṃ prajñāvādāṃś ca bhāṣase / gatāsūn agatāsūṃś ca nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: You grieve for those who should not be grieved for, yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise grieve neither for the living nor for the dead.

verses.transliteration: na tv evāhaṃ jātu nāsaṃ na tvaṃ neme janādhipāḥ / na caiva na bhaviṣyāmaḥ sarve vayam ataḥ param /
verses.translation: Never was there a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor all these beings; nor will there be any time when we shall cease to exist.

verses.transliteration: dehino 'smin yathā dehe kaumāraṃ yauvanaṃ jarā / tathā dehāntaraprāptir dhīras tatra na muhyati /
verses.translation: Just as the embodied soul passes through childhood, youth, and old age in this body, so too does it pass into another body. The wise man is not confused by this change.

verses.transliteration: mātrāsparśās tu kaunteya śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkhadāḥ / āgamāpāyino 'nityās tāṃs titikṣasva bhārata /
verses.translation: The contacts of the senses with their objects, which cause heat and cold, pleasure and pain, O Arjuna, have a beginning and an end. They are impermanent. Endure them, O Arjuna.

verses.transliteration: yaṃ hi na vyathayanty ete puruṣaṃ puruṣarṣabha / samaduḥkhasukhaṃ dhīraṃ so 'mṛtatvāya kalpate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 15: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nāsato vidyate bhāvo nābhāvo vidyate sataḥ / ubhayor api dṛṣṭo 'ntas tv anayos tattvadarśibhiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 16: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: avināśi tu tad viddhi yena sarvam idaṃ tatam / vināśam avyayasyāsya na kaś cit kartum arhati /
verses.translation: Know that which pervades the entire body is indestructible. No one is able to destroy the imperishable soul.

verses.transliteration: antavanta ime dehā nityasyoktāḥ śarīriṇaḥ / anāśino 'prameyasya tasmād yudhyasva bhārata /
verses.translation: The material body of the indestructible, immeasurable, and eternal living entity is bound to come to an end; therefore, fight, O descendant of Bharata.

verses.transliteration: ya enaṃ vetti hantāraṃ yaś cainaṃ manyate hatam / ubhau tau na vijānīto nāyaṃ hanti na hanyate /
verses.translation: He who thinks that the living entity is the slayer, and he who thinks that the entity is slain, are both without knowledge, for the self neither slays nor is slain.

verses.transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadā cin; nāyaṃ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ / ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṃ purāṇo; na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre /
verses.translation: The soul is never born nor dies at any time. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. It is unborn, eternal, ever-existing, and primeval. It is not slain when the body is slain.

verses.transliteration: vedāvināśinaṃ nityaṃ ya enam ajam avyayam / kathaṃ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṃ ghātayati hanti kam /
verses.translation: O Partha, how can a person who knows that the soul is indestructible, eternal, unborn, and immutable kill anyone or cause anyone to kill?

verses.transliteration: vāsāṃsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya; navāni gṛhṇāti naro 'parāṇi / tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāni; anyāni saṃyāti navāni dehī /
verses.translation: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.

verses.transliteration: nainaṃ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṃ dahati pāvakaḥ / na cainaṃ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ /
verses.translation: The soul can never be cut to pieces by any weapon, nor burned by fire, nor moistened by water, nor withered by the wind.

verses.transliteration: acchedyo 'yam adāhyo 'yam akledyo 'śoṣya eva ca / nityaḥ sarvagataḥ sthāṇur acalo 'yaṃ sanātanaḥ /
verses.translation: This individual soul is unbreakable and insoluble, and can be neither burned nor dried. It is everlasting, present everywhere, unchangeable, immovable, and eternally the same.

verses.transliteration: avyakto 'yam acintyo 'yam avikāryo 'yam ucyate / tasmād evaṃ viditvainaṃ nānuśocitum arhasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 25: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: atha cainaṃ nityajātaṃ nityaṃ vā manyase mṛtam / tathāpi tvaṃ mahābāho naivaṃ śocitum arhasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 26: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛtyur dhruvaṃ janma mṛtasya ca / tasmād aparihārye 'rthe na tvaṃ śocitum arhasi /
verses.translation: One who has taken his birth is sure to die, and after death one is sure to take birth again. Therefore, in the unavoidable discharge of your duty, you should not lament.

verses.transliteration: avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyaktamadhyāni bhārata / avyaktanidhanāny eva tatra kā paridevanā /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 28: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āścaryavat paśyati kaś cid enam; āścaryavad vadati tathaiva cānyaḥ / āścaryavac cainam anyaḥ śṛṇoti; śrutvāpy enaṃ veda na caiva kaś cit /
verses.translation: Some look on the soul as amazing, some describe it as amazing, and some hear of it as amazing, while others, even after hearing about it, cannot understand it at all.

verses.transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo 'yaṃ dehe sarvasya bhārata / tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṃ śocitum arhasi /
verses.translation: O descendant of Bharata, the soul dwelling in the body can never be slain. Therefore you need not grieve for any creature.

verses.transliteration: svadharmam api cāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi / dharmyād dhi yuddhāc chreyo 'nyat kṣatriyasya na vidyate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 31: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadṛcchayā copapannaṃ svargadvāram apāvṛtam / sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛśam /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 32: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: atha cet tvam imaṃ dharmyaṃ saṃgrāmaṃ na kariṣyasi / tataḥ svadharmaṃ kīrtiṃ ca hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 33: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: akīrtiṃ cāpi bhūtāni kathayiṣyanti te 'vyayām / saṃbhāvitasya cākīrtir maraṇād atiricyate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 34: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhayād raṇād uparataṃ maṃsyante tvāṃ mahārathāḥ / yeṣāṃ ca tvaṃ bahumato bhūtvā yāsyasi lāghavam /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 35: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: avācyavādāṃś ca bahūn vadiṣyanti tavāhitāḥ / nindantas tava sāmarthyaṃ tato duḥkhataraṃ nu kim /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 36: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: hato vā prāpsyasi svargaṃ jitvā vā bhokṣyase mahīm / tasmād uttiṣṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛtaniścayaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 37: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sukhaduḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhālābhau jayājayau / tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṃ pāpam avāpsyasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 38: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: eṣā te 'bhihitā sāṃkhye buddhir yoge tv imāṃ śṛṇu / buddhyā yukto yayā pārtha karmabandhaṃ prahāsyasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 39: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nehābhikramanāśo 'sti pratyavāyo na vidyate / svalpam apy asya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt /
verses.translation: In this endeavour there is no loss or diminution, and a little advancement on this path can protect one from the most dangerous type of fear.

verses.transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kurunandana / bahuśākhā hy anantāś ca buddhayo 'vyavasāyinām /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 41: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yām imāṃ puṣpitāṃ vācaṃ pravadanty avipaścitaḥ / vedavādaratāḥ pārtha nānyad astīti vādinaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 42: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāmātmānaḥ svargaparā janmakarmaphalapradām / kriyāviśeṣabahulāṃ bhogaiśvaryagatiṃ prati /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 43: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhogaiśvaryaprasaktānāṃ tayāpahṛtacetasām / vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 44: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: traiguṇyaviṣayā vedā nistraiguṇyo bhavārjuna / nirdvandvo nityasattvastho niryogakṣema ātmavān /
verses.translation: The Vedas deal mainly with the subject of the three modes of material nature. O Arjuna, become transcendental to these three modes. Be free from all dualities and from all anxieties for gain and safety, and be established in the self.

verses.transliteration: yāvān artha udapāne sarvataḥ saṃplutodake / tāvān sarveṣu vedeṣu brāhmaṇasya vijānataḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 46: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadā cana / mā karmaphalahetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi /
verses.translation: You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.

verses.transliteration: yogasthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā dhanaṃjaya / siddhyasiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṃ yoga ucyate /
verses.translation: Perform your duty equipoised, O Arjuna, abandoning all attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.

verses.transliteration: dūreṇa hy avaraṃ karma buddhiyogād dhanaṃjaya / buddhau śaraṇam anviccha kṛpaṇāḥ phalahetavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 49: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: buddhiyukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛtaduṣkṛte / tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam /
verses.translation: A man engaged in devotional service rids himself of both good and bad actions even in this life. Therefore strive for yoga, which is the art of all work.

verses.transliteration: karmajaṃ buddhiyuktā hi phalaṃ tyaktvā manīṣiṇaḥ / janmabandhavinirmuktāḥ padaṃ gacchanty anāmayam /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 51: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā te mohakalilaṃ buddhir vyatitariṣyati / tadā gantāsi nirvedaṃ śrotavyasya śrutasya ca /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 52: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrutivipratipannā te yadā sthāsyati niścalā / samādhāv acalā buddhis tadā yogam avāpsyasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 53: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / sthitaprajñasya kā bhāṣā samādhisthasya keśava / sthitadhīḥ kiṃ prabhāṣeta kim āsīta vrajeta kim /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 54: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prajahāti yadā kāmān sarvān pārtha manogatān / ātmany evātmanā tuṣṭaḥ sthitaprajñas tadocyate /
verses.translation: O Arjuna, when a man gives up all varieties of desire for sense gratification, which arise from mental concoction, and when his mind finds satisfaction in the self alone, then he is said to be in pure transcendental consciousness.

verses.transliteration: duḥkheṣv anudvignamanāḥ sukheṣu vigataspṛhaḥ / vītarāgabhayakrodhaḥ sthitadhīr munir ucyate /
verses.translation: One who is not disturbed in mind even amidst the threefold miseries or elated when there is happiness, and who is free from attachment, fear, and anger, is called a sage of steady mind.

verses.transliteration: yaḥ sarvatrānabhisnehas tat tat prāpya śubhāśubham / nābhinandati na dveṣṭi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 57: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā saṃharate cāyaṃ kūrmo 'ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ / indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā /
verses.translation: One who is able to withdraw his senses from sense objects, as the tortoise draws its limbs within the shell, is firmly fixed in perfect consciousness.

verses.transliteration: viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ / rasavarjaṃ raso 'py asya paraṃ dṛṣṭvā nivartate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 59: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ / indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṃ manaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 60: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tāni sarvāṇi saṃyamya yukta āsīta matparaḥ / vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 61: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dhyāyato viṣayān puṃsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate / saṅgāt saṃjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho 'bhijāyate /
verses.translation: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.

verses.transliteration: krodhād bhavati saṃmohaḥ saṃmohāt smṛtivibhramaḥ / smṛtibhraṃśād buddhināśo buddhināśāt praṇaśyati /
verses.translation: From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.

verses.transliteration: rāgadveṣaviyuktais tu viṣayān indriyaiś caran / ātmavaśyair vidheyātmā prasādam adhigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 64: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prasāde sarvaduḥkhānāṃ hānir asyopajāyate / prasannacetaso hy āśu buddhiḥ paryavatiṣṭhate /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 65: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na cāyuktasya bhāvanā / na cābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham /
verses.translation: One who is not connected with the Supreme can have neither transcendental intelligence nor a steady mind, without which there is no possibility of peace. And how can there be any happiness without peace?

verses.transliteration: indriyāṇāṃ hi caratāṃ yan mano 'nuvidhīyate / tad asya harati prajñāṃ vāyur nāvam ivāmbhasi /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 67: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tasmād yasya mahābāho nigṛhītāni sarvaśaḥ / indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 68: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yā niśā sarvabhūtānāṃ tasyāṃ jāgarti saṃyamī / yasyāṃ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ /
verses.translation: In the Sankhya Yoga (Chapter 2), verse 69: Krishna begins his teaching. He explains the eternal nature of the soul, the path of selfless action, and the qualities of a stable-minded sage. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āpūryamāṇam acalapratiṣṭhaṃ; samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat / tadvat kāmā yaṃ praviśanti sarve; sa śāntim āpnoti na kāmakāmī /
verses.translation: A person who is not disturbed by the incessant flow of desires — that enter like rivers into the ocean, which is ever being filled but is always still — can alone achieve peace, and not the man who strives to satisfy such desires.

verses.transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṃś carati niḥspṛhaḥ / nirmamo nirahaṃkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati /
verses.translation: A person who has given up all desires for sense gratification, who lives free from desires, who has given up all sense of proprietorship and is devoid of false ego — he alone can attain real peace.

verses.transliteration: eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṃ prāpya vimuhyati / sthitvāsyām antakāle 'pi brahmanirvāṇam ṛcchati /
verses.translation: That is the way of the spiritual and godly life, after attaining which a man is not bewildered. If one is thus situated even at the hour of death, one can enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / jyāyasī cet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana / tat kiṃ karmaṇi ghore māṃ niyojayasi keśava /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 1: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vyāmiśreṇaiva vākyena buddhiṃ mohayasīva me / tad ekaṃ vada niścitya yena śreyo 'ham āpnuyām /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 2: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / loke 'smin dvividhā niṣṭhā purā proktā mayānagha / jñānayogena sāṃkhyānāṃ karmayogena yoginām /
verses.translation: O sinless Arjuna, I have already explained that there are two classes of men who realise the Self. Some are inclined to understand it by empirical, philosophical speculation, and others are inclined to understand it by devotional work.

verses.transliteration: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṃ puruṣo 'śnute / na ca saṃnyasanād eva siddhiṃ samadhigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 4: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na hi kaś cit kṣaṇam api jātu tiṣṭhaty akarmakṛt / kāryate hy avaśaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛtijair guṇaiḥ /
verses.translation: Everyone is forced to act helplessly according to the qualities he has acquired from the modes of material nature; therefore no one can refrain from doing something, not even for a moment.

verses.transliteration: karmendriyāṇi saṃyamya ya āste manasā smaran / indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyācāraḥ sa ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 6: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate 'rjuna / karmendriyaiḥ karmayogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 7: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: niyataṃ kuru karma tvaṃ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ / śarīrayātrāpi ca te na prasidhyed akarmaṇaḥ /
verses.translation: Perform your prescribed duty, for doing so is better than not working. One cannot even maintain one's physical body without work.

verses.transliteration: yajñārthāt karmaṇo 'nyatra loko 'yaṃ karmabandhanaḥ / tadarthaṃ karma kaunteya muktasaṅgaḥ samācara /
verses.translation: Work done as a sacrifice for Vishnu has to be performed, otherwise work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kunti, perform your prescribed duties for His satisfaction, and in that way you will always remain unattached and free from bondage.

verses.transliteration: sahayajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā purovāca prajāpatiḥ / anena prasaviṣyadhvam eṣa vo 'stv iṣṭakāmadhuk /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 10: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ / parasparaṃ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 11: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajñabhāvitāḥ / tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 12: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yajñaśiṣṭāśinaḥ santo mucyante sarvakilbiṣaiḥ / bhuñjate te tv aghaṃ pāpā ye pacanty ātmakāraṇāt /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 13: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād annasaṃbhavaḥ / yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karmasamudbhavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 14: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: karma brahmodbhavaṃ viddhi brahmākṣarasamudbhavam / tasmāt sarvagataṃ brahma nityaṃ yajñe pratiṣṭhitam /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 15: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: evaṃ pravartitaṃ cakraṃ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ / aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṃ pārtha sa jīvati /
verses.translation: My dear Arjuna, one who does not follow in human life the cycle of sacrifice thus established by the Vedas certainly leads a life full of sin. Living only for the satisfaction of the senses, such a person lives in vain.

verses.transliteration: yas tv ātmaratir eva syād ātmatṛptaś ca mānavaḥ / ātmany eva ca saṃtuṣṭas tasya kāryaṃ na vidyate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 17: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: naiva tasya kṛtenārtho nākṛteneha kaś cana / na cāsya sarvabhūteṣu kaś cid arthavyapāśrayaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 18: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṃ kāryaṃ karma samācara / asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ /
verses.translation: Therefore, without being attached to the fruits of activities, one should act as a matter of duty, for by working without attachment one attains the Supreme.

verses.transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi saṃsiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ / lokasaṃgraham evāpi saṃpaśyan kartum arhasi /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 20: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ / sa yat pramāṇaṃ kurute lokas tad anuvartate /
verses.translation: Whatever action a great man performs, common men follow in his footsteps. And whatever standards he sets by exemplary acts, all the world pursues.

verses.transliteration: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṃ triṣu lokeṣu kiṃ cana / nānavāptam avāptavyaṃ varta eva ca karmaṇi /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 22: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadi hy ahaṃ na varteyaṃ jātu karmaṇy atandritaḥ / mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 23: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṃ karma ced aham / saṃkarasya ca kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 24: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṃso yathā kurvanti bhārata / kuryād vidvāṃs tathāsaktaś cikīrṣur lokasaṃgraham /
verses.translation: As the ignorant perform their duties with attachment to results, the learned may similarly act, but without attachment, for the sake of leading people on the right path.

verses.transliteration: na buddhibhedaṃ janayed ajñānāṃ karmasaṅginām / joṣayet sarvakarmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācaran /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 26: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ / ahaṃkāravimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate /
verses.translation: The bewildered spirit soul, under the influence of the three modes of material nature, thinks himself to be the doer of activities that are in actuality carried out by nature.

verses.transliteration: tattvavit tu mahābāho guṇakarmavibhāgayoḥ / guṇā guṇeṣu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 28: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prakṛter guṇasaṃmūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇakarmasu / tān akṛtsnavido mandān kṛtsnavin na vicālayet /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 29: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi saṃnyasyādhyātmacetasā / nirāśīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigatajvaraḥ /
verses.translation: Therefore, O Arjuna, surrendering all your works unto Me, with full knowledge of Me, without desires for profit, with no claims to proprietorship, and free from lethargy, fight.

verses.transliteration: ye me matam idaṃ nityam anutiṣṭhanti mānavāḥ / śraddhāvanto 'nasūyanto mucyante te 'pi karmabhiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 31: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ye tv etad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣṭhanti me matam / sarvajñānavimūḍhāṃs tān viddhi naṣṭān acetasaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 32: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sadṛśaṃ ceṣṭate svasyāḥ prakṛter jñānavān api / prakṛtiṃ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṃ kariṣyati /
verses.translation: Even a man of knowledge acts according to his own nature, for everyone follows the nature he has acquired from the three modes. What can repression accomplish?

verses.transliteration: indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāgadveṣau vyavasthitau / tayor na vaśam āgacchet tau hy asya paripanthinau /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 34: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt / svadharme nidhanaṃ śreyaḥ paradharmo bhayāvahaḥ /
verses.translation: It is far better to discharge one's prescribed duties, even though faultily, than another's duties perfectly. Destruction in the course of performing one's own duty is better than engaging in another's duties, for to follow another's path is dangerous.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / atha kena prayukto 'yaṃ pāpaṃ carati pūruṣaḥ / anicchann api vārṣṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: O descendant of Vrishni, by what is one impelled to sinful acts, even unwillingly, as if engaged by force?

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / kāma eṣa krodha eṣa rajoguṇasamudbhavaḥ / mahāśano mahāpāpmā viddhy enam iha vairiṇam /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: It is lust only, Arjuna, which is born of contact with the material mode of passion and later transformed into wrath, and which is the all-devouring sinful enemy of this world.

verses.transliteration: dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśo malena ca / yatholbenāvṛto garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 38: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āvṛtaṃ jñānam etena jñānino nityavairiṇā / kāmarūpeṇa kaunteya duṣpūreṇānalena ca /
verses.translation: In the Karma Yoga (Chapter 3), verse 39: Krishna teaches Karma Yoga: one must act without attachment to results. Desire is the enemy; selfless action as sacrifice is the path. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣṭhānam ucyate / etair vimohayaty eṣa jñānam āvṛtya dehinam /
verses.translation: The senses, the mind, and the intelligence are the sitting places of this lust. Through them lust covers the real knowledge of the living entity and bewilders him.

verses.transliteration: tasmāt tvam indriyāṇy ādau niyamya bharatarṣabha / pāpmānaṃ prajahi hy enaṃ jñānavijñānanāśanam /
verses.translation: Therefore, O Arjuna, best of the Bharatas, in the very beginning curb this great symbol of sin (lust) by regulating the senses, and slay this destroyer of knowledge and self-realisation.

verses.transliteration: indriyāṇi parāṇy āhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṃ manaḥ / manasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ /
verses.translation: The working senses are superior to dull matter; mind is higher than the senses; intelligence is still higher than the mind; and the soul is even higher than intelligence.

verses.transliteration: evaṃ buddheḥ paraṃ buddhvā saṃstabhyātmānam ātmanā / jahi śatruṃ mahābāho kāmarūpaṃ durāsadam /
verses.translation: Thus knowing oneself to be transcendental to the material senses, mind, and intelligence, O mighty-armed Arjuna, one should steady the mind by deliberate spiritual intelligence and thus — by spiritual strength — conquer this insatiable enemy known as lust.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / imaṃ vivasvate yogaṃ proktavān aham avyayam / vivasvān manave prāha manur ikṣvākave 'bravīt /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: I instructed this imperishable science of yoga to the sun god, Vivasvan, and Vivasvan instructed it to Manu, the father of mankind, and Manu in turn instructed it to Ikshvaku.

verses.transliteration: evaṃ paramparāprāptam imaṃ rājarṣayo viduḥ / sa kāleneha mahatā yogo naṣṭaḥ paraṃtapa /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 2: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sa evāyaṃ mayā te 'dya yogaḥ proktaḥ purātanaḥ / bhakto 'si me sakhā ceti rahasyaṃ hy etad uttamam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 3: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / aparaṃ bhavato janma paraṃ janma vivasvataḥ / katham etad vijānīyāṃ tvam ādau proktavān iti /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 4: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / bahūni me vyatītāni janmāni tava cārjuna / tāny ahaṃ veda sarvāṇi na tvaṃ vettha paraṃtapa /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 5: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ajo 'pi sann avyayātmā bhūtānām īśvaro 'pi san / prakṛtiṃ svām adhiṣṭhāya saṃbhavāmy ātmamāyayā /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 6: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata / abhyutthānam adharmasya tadātmānaṃ sṛjāmy aham /
verses.translation: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion — at that time I descend Myself.

verses.transliteration: paritrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ vināśāya ca duṣkṛtām / dharmasaṃsthāpanārthāya saṃbhavāmi yuge yuge /
verses.translation: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.

verses.transliteration: janma karma ca me divyam evaṃ yo vetti tattvataḥ / tyaktvā dehaṃ punarjanma naiti mām eti so 'rjuna /
verses.translation: One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.

verses.transliteration: vītarāgabhayakrodhā manmayā mām upāśritāḥ / bahavo jñānatapasā pūtā madbhāvam āgatāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 10: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ye yathā māṃ prapadyante tāṃs tathaiva bhajāmy aham / mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ /
verses.translation: As all surrender unto Me, I reward them accordingly. Everyone follows My path in all respects, O son of Pritha.

verses.transliteration: kāṅkṣantaḥ karmaṇāṃ siddhiṃ yajanta iha devatāḥ / kṣipraṃ hi mānuṣe loke siddhir bhavati karmajā /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 12: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: cāturvarṇyaṃ mayā sṛṣṭaṃ guṇakarmavibhāgaśaḥ / tasya kartāram api māṃ viddhy akartāram avyayam /
verses.translation: According to the three modes of material nature and the work associated with them, the four divisions of human society are created by Me. And although I am the creator of this system, you should know that I am yet the nondoer, being unchangeable.

verses.transliteration: na māṃ karmāṇi limpanti na me karmaphale spṛhā / iti māṃ yo 'bhijānāti karmabhir na sa badhyate /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 14: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: evaṃ jñātvā kṛtaṃ karma pūrvair api mumukṣubhiḥ / kuru karmaiva tasmāt tvaṃ pūrvaiḥ pūrvataraṃ kṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 15: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kiṃ karma kimakarmeti kavayo 'py atra mohitāḥ / tat te karma pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvā mokṣyase 'śubhāt /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 16: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: karmaṇo hy api boddhavyaṃ boddhavyaṃ ca vikarmaṇaḥ / akarmaṇaś ca boddhavyaṃ gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 17: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: karmaṇy akarma yaḥ paśyed akarmaṇi ca karma yaḥ / sa buddhimān manuṣyeṣu sa yuktaḥ kṛtsnakarmakṛt /
verses.translation: One who sees inaction in action, and action in inaction, is intelligent among men, and he is in the transcendental position, although engaged in all sorts of activities.

verses.transliteration: yasya sarve samārambhāḥ kāmasaṃkalpavarjitāḥ / jñānāgnidagdhakarmāṇaṃ tam āhuḥ paṇḍitaṃ budhāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 19: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tyaktvā karmaphalāsaṅgaṃ nityatṛpto nirāśrayaḥ / karmaṇy abhipravṛtto 'pi naiva kiṃ cit karoti saḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 20: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nirāśīr yatacittātmā tyaktasarvaparigrahaḥ / śārīraṃ kevalaṃ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 21: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadṛcchālābhasaṃtuṣṭo dvandvātīto vimatsaraḥ / samaḥ siddhāv asiddhau ca kṛtvāpi na nibadhyate /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 22: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: gatasaṅgasya muktasya jñānāvasthitacetasaḥ / yajñāyācarataḥ karma samagraṃ pravilīyate /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 23: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: brahmārpaṇaṃ brahma havir brahmāgnau brahmaṇā hutam / brahmaiva tena gantavyaṃ brahmakarmasamādhinā /
verses.translation: A person who is fully absorbed in Krishna consciousness is sure to attain the spiritual kingdom because of his full contribution to spiritual activities, in which the consummation is absolute and that which is offered is of the same spiritual nature.

verses.transliteration: daivam evāpare yajñaṃ yoginaḥ paryupāsate / brahmāgnāv apare yajñaṃ yajñenaivopajuhvati /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 25: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrotrādīnīndriyāṇy anye saṃyamāgniṣu juhvati / śabdādīn viṣayān anya indriyāgniṣu juhvati /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 26: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvāṇīndriyakarmāṇi prāṇakarmāṇi cāpare / ātmasaṃyamayogāgnau juhvati jñānadīpite /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 27: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dravyayajñās tapoyajñā yogayajñās tathāpare / svādhyāyajñānayajñāś ca yatayaḥ saṃśitavratāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 28: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: apāne juhvati prāṇaṃ prāṇe 'pānaṃ tathāpare / prāṇāpānagatī ruddhvā prāṇāyāmaparāyaṇāḥ /
verses.translation: Still others, who are inclined to the process of breath restraint to remain in trance, practice by offering the movement of the outgoing breath into the incoming, and the incoming breath into the outgoing, and thus at last remain in trance, stopping all breathing.

verses.transliteration: apare niyatāhārāḥ prāṇān prāṇeṣu juhvati / sarve 'py ete yajñavido yajñakṣapitakalmaṣāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 30: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yajñaśiṣṭāmṛtabhujo yānti brahma sanātanam / nāyaṃ loko 'sty ayajñasya kuto 'nyaḥ kurusattama /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 31: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: evaṃ bahuvidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe / karmajān viddhi tān sarvān evaṃ jñātvā vimokṣyase /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 32: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śreyān dravyamayād yajñāj jñānayajñaḥ paraṃtapa / sarvaṃ karmākhilaṃ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate /
verses.translation: O chastiser of the enemy, the sacrifice performed in knowledge is better than the mere sacrifice of material possessions. After all, O son of Pritha, all sacrifices of work culminate in transcendental knowledge.

verses.transliteration: tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśnena sevayā / upadekṣyanti te jñānaṃ jñāninas tattvadarśinaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 34: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṃ yāsyasi pāṇḍava / yena bhūtāny aśeṣeṇa drakṣyasy ātmany atho mayi /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 35: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: api ced asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpakṛttamaḥ / sarvaṃ jñānaplavenaiva vṛjinaṃ saṃtariṣyasi /
verses.translation: Even if you are considered to be the most sinful of all sinners, when you are situated in the boat of transcendental knowledge you will be able to cross over the ocean of miseries.

verses.transliteration: yathaidhāṃsi samiddho 'gnir bhasmasāt kurute 'rjuna / jñānāgniḥ sarvakarmāṇi bhasmasāt kurute tathā /
verses.translation: As the blazing fire turns firewood to ashes, O Arjuna, so does the fire of knowledge burn to ashes all reactions to material activities.

verses.transliteration: na hi jñānena sadṛśaṃ pavitram iha vidyate / tat svayaṃ yogasaṃsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati /
verses.translation: In this world, there is nothing so sublime and pure as transcendental knowledge. Such knowledge is the mature fruit of all mysticism. And one who has become accomplished in the practice of devotional service enjoys this knowledge within himself in due course of time.

verses.transliteration: śraddhāvāṁl labhate jñānaṃ tatparaḥ saṃyatendriyaḥ / jñānaṃ labdhvā parāṃ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati /
verses.translation: A faithful man who is dedicated to transcendental knowledge and who subdues his senses is eligible to achieve such knowledge, and having achieved it he quickly attains the supreme spiritual peace.

verses.transliteration: ajñaś cāśraddadhānaś ca saṃśayātmā vinaśyati / nāyaṃ loko 'sti na paro na sukhaṃ saṃśayātmanaḥ /
verses.translation: But ignorant and faithless persons who doubt the revealed scriptures do not attain God consciousness; they fall down. For the doubting soul there is happiness neither in this world nor in the next.

verses.transliteration: yogasaṃnyastakarmāṇaṃ jñānasaṃchinnasaṃśayam / ātmavantaṃ na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanaṃjaya /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Karma Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 4), verse 41: Krishna reveals the divine mystery of his repeated incarnations and teaches that all actions become sacred when performed with wisdom and offered to the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tasmād ajñānasaṃbhūtaṃ hṛtsthaṃ jñānāsinātmanaḥ / chittvainaṃ saṃśayaṃ yogam ātiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhārata /
verses.translation: Therefore the doubts which have arisen in your heart out of ignorance should be slashed by the weapon of knowledge. Armed with yoga, O Bharata, stand and fight.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / saṃnyāsaṃ karmaṇāṃ kṛṣṇa punar yogaṃ ca śaṃsasi / yac chreya etayor ekaṃ tan me brūhi suniścitam /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 1: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / saṃnyāsaḥ karmayogaś ca niḥśreyasakarāv ubhau / tayos tu karmasaṃnyāsāt karmayogo viśiṣyate /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: The renunciation of work and work in devotion are both good for liberation. But, of the two, work in devotional service is better than renunciation of work.

verses.transliteration: jñeyaḥ sa nityasaṃnyāsī yo na dveṣṭi na kāṅkṣati / nirdvandvo hi mahābāho sukhaṃ bandhāt pramucyate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 3: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sāṃkhyayogau pṛthag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ / ekam apy āsthitaḥ samyag ubhayor vindate phalam /
verses.translation: Only the ignorant speak of devotional service as being different from the analytical study of the material world. Those who are actually learned say that he who applies himself well to one of these paths achieves the results of both.

verses.transliteration: yat sāṃkhyaiḥ prāpyate sthānaṃ tad yogair api gamyate / ekaṃ sāṃkhyaṃ ca yogaṃ ca yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 5: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃnyāsas tu mahābāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥ / yogayukto munir brahma nacireṇādhigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 6: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yogayukto viśuddhātmā vijitātmā jitendriyaḥ / sarvabhūtātmabhūtātmā kurvann api na lipyate /
verses.translation: One who works in devotion, who is a pure soul, and who controls his mind and senses is dear to everyone, and everyone is dear to him. Though always working, such a man is never entangled.

verses.transliteration: naiva kiṃ cit karomīti yukto manyeta tattvavit / paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañ jighrann aśnan gacchan svapañ śvasan /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 8: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇann unmiṣan nimiṣann api / indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 9: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ / lipyate na sa pāpena padmapatram ivāmbhasā /
verses.translation: One who performs his duty without attachment, surrendering the results unto the Supreme Lord, is unaffected by sinful action, as the lotus leaf is untouched by water.

verses.transliteration: kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api / yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṃ tyaktvātmaśuddhaye /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 11: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yuktaḥ karmaphalaṃ tyaktvā śāntim āpnoti naiṣṭhikīm / ayuktaḥ kāmakāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 12: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvakarmāṇi manasā saṃnyasyāste sukhaṃ vaśī / navadvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan /
verses.translation: When the embodied living being controls his nature and mentally renounces all actions, he resides happily in the city of nine gates (the material body), neither working nor causing work to be done.

verses.transliteration: na kartṛtvaṃ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ / na karmaphalasaṃyogaṃ svabhāvas tu pravartate /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 14: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nādatte kasya cit pāpaṃ na caiva sukṛtaṃ vibhuḥ / ajñānenāvṛtaṃ jñānaṃ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 15: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jñānena tu tad ajñānaṃ yeṣāṃ nāśitam ātmanaḥ / teṣām ādityavaj jñānaṃ prakāśayati tatparam /
verses.translation: When, however, one is enlightened with the knowledge by which nescience is destroyed, then his knowledge reveals everything, as the sun lights up everything in the daytime.

verses.transliteration: tadbuddhayas tadātmānas tanniṣṭhās tatparāyaṇāḥ / gacchanty apunarāvṛttiṃ jñānanirdhūtakalmaṣāḥ /
verses.translation: When one's intelligence, mind, faith, and refuge are all fixed in the Supreme, then one becomes fully cleansed of misgivings through complete knowledge and thus proceeds straight on the path of liberation.

verses.transliteration: vidyāvinayasaṃpanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini / śuni caiva śvapāke ca paṇḍitāḥ samadarśinaḥ /
