verses.translation: The humble sages, by virtue of true knowledge, see with equal vision a learned and gentle brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and a dog-eater.

verses.transliteration: ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣāṃ sāmye sthitaṃ manaḥ / nirdoṣaṃ hi samaṃ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ /
verses.translation: Those whose minds are established in sameness and equanimity have already conquered the conditions of birth and death. They are flawless like Brahman, and thus they are already situated in Brahman.

verses.transliteration: na prahṛṣyet priyaṃ prāpya nodvijet prāpya cāpriyam / sthirabuddhir asaṃmūḍho brahmavid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 20: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bāhyasparśeṣv asaktātmā vindaty ātmani yat sukham / sa brahmayogayuktātmā sukham akṣayam aśnute /
verses.translation: Such a liberated person is not attracted to material sense pleasure but is always in trance, enjoying the pleasure within. In this way the self-realised person enjoys unlimited happiness, for he concentrates on the Supreme.

verses.transliteration: ye hi saṃsparśajā bhogā duḥkhayonaya eva te / ādyantavantaḥ kaunteya na teṣu ramate budhaḥ /
verses.translation: An intelligent person does not take part in the sources of misery, which are due to contact with the material senses. O son of Kunti, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, and so the wise man does not delight in them.

verses.transliteration: śaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhuṃ prāk śarīravimokṣaṇāt / kāmakrodhodbhavaṃ vegaṃ sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 23: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yo 'ntaḥsukho 'ntarārāmas tathāntarjyotir eva yaḥ / sa yogī brahmanirvāṇaṃ brahmabhūto 'dhigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 24: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: labhante brahmanirvāṇam ṛṣayaḥ kṣīṇakalmaṣāḥ / chinnadvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarvabhūtahite ratāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 25: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāmakrodhaviyuktānāṃ yatīnāṃ yatacetasām / abhito brahmanirvāṇaṃ vartate viditātmanām /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 26: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sparśān kṛtvā bahir bāhyāṃś cakṣuś caivāntare bhruvoḥ / prāṇāpānau samau kṛtvā nāsābhyantaracāriṇau /
verses.translation: Shutting out all external sense objects, keeping the eyes and vision concentrated between the two eyebrows, suspending the inward and outward breaths within the nostrils, and thus controlling the mind, senses, and intelligence.

verses.transliteration: yatendriyamanobuddhir munir mokṣaparāyaṇaḥ / vigatecchābhayakrodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ /
verses.translation: In the Karma Vairagya Yoga (Chapter 5), verse 28: Both renunciation and selfless action lead to liberation, but Krishna recommends action. The true renunciant acts without being bound by action. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhoktāraṃ yajñatapasāṃ sarvalokamaheśvaram / suhṛdaṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ jñātvā māṃ śāntim ṛcchati /
verses.translation: A person in full consciousness of Me, knowing Me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods, and the benefactor and well-wisher of all living entities, attains peace from the pangs of material miseries.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / anāśritaḥ karmaphalaṃ kāryaṃ karma karoti yaḥ / sa saṃnyāsī ca yogī ca na niragnir na cākriyaḥ /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: One who is unattached to the fruits of his work and who works as he is obligated is in the renounced order of life, and he is the true mystic, not he who lights no fire and performs no duty.

verses.transliteration: yaṃ saṃnyāsam iti prāhur yogaṃ taṃ viddhi pāṇḍava / na hy asaṃnyastasaṃkalpo yogī bhavati kaś cana /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 2: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ārurukṣor muner yogaṃ karma kāraṇam ucyate / yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śamaḥ kāraṇam ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 3: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate / sarvasaṃkalpasaṃnyāsī yogārūḍhas tadocyate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 4: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: uddhared ātmanātmānaṃ nātmānam avasādayet / ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ /
verses.translation: One must deliver himself with the help of his mind, and not degrade himself. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.

verses.transliteration: bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ / anātmanas tu śatrutve vartetātmaiva śatruvat /
verses.translation: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his mind will remain the greatest enemy.

verses.transliteration: jitātmanaḥ praśāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ / śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkheṣu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 7: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jñānavijñānatṛptātmā kūṭastho vijitendriyaḥ / yukta ity ucyate yogī samaloṣṭāśmakāñcanaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 8: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: suhṛnmitrāryudāsīnamadhyasthadveṣyabandhuṣu / sādhuṣv api ca pāpeṣu samabuddhir viśiṣyate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 9: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṃ rahasi sthitaḥ / ekākī yatacittātmā nirāśīr aparigrahaḥ /
verses.translation: A transcendentalist should always engage his body, mind, and self in relationship with the Supreme; he should live alone in a secluded place and should always carefully control his mind. He should be free from desires and feelings of possessiveness.

verses.transliteration: śucau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ / nātyucchritaṃ nātinīcaṃ cailājinakuśottaram /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 11: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tatraikāgraṃ manaḥ kṛtvā yatacittendriyakriyaḥ / upaviśyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātmaviśuddhaye /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 12: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: samaṃ kāyaśirogrīvaṃ dhārayann acalaṃ sthiraḥ / saṃprekṣya nāsikāgraṃ svaṃ diśaś cānavalokayan /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 13: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: praśāntātmā vigatabhīr brahmacārivrate sthitaḥ / manaḥ saṃyamya maccitto yukta āsīta matparaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 14: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yuñjann evaṃ sadātmānaṃ yogī niyatamānasaḥ / śāntiṃ nirvāṇaparamāṃ matsaṃsthām adhigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 15: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nātyaśnatas tu yogo 'sti na caikāntam anaśnataḥ / na cātisvapnaśīlasya jāgrato naiva cārjuna /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 16: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yuktāhāravihārasya yuktaceṣṭasya karmasu / yuktasvapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkhahā /
verses.translation: He who is regulated in his habits of eating, sleeping, recreation, and work can mitigate all material pains by practising the yoga system.

verses.transliteration: yadā viniyataṃ cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate / niḥspṛhaḥ sarvakāmebhyo yukta ity ucyate tadā /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 18: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathā dīpo nivātastho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā / yogino yatacittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ /
verses.translation: As a lamp in a windless place does not waver, so the transcendentalist, whose mind is controlled, remains always steady in his meditation on the transcendent Self.

verses.transliteration: yatroparamate cittaṃ niruddhaṃ yogasevayā / yatra caivātmanātmānaṃ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati /
verses.translation: In the stage of perfection called trance, or samadhi, one's mind is completely restrained from material mental activities by practice of yoga. This perfection is characterised by one's ability to see the self by the pure mind and to relish and rejoice in the self.

verses.transliteration: sukham ātyantikaṃ yat tad buddhigrāhyam atīndriyam / vetti yatra na caivāyaṃ sthitaś calati tattvataḥ /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 21: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yaṃ labdhvā cāparaṃ lābhaṃ manyate nādhikaṃ tataḥ / yasmin sthito na duḥkhena guruṇāpi vicālyate /
verses.translation: Upon gaining this, one thinks that there is no greater gain. Being situated in such a position, one is never shaken, even in the midst of the greatest difficulty.

verses.transliteration: taṃ vidyād duḥkhasaṃyogaviyogaṃ yogasaṃjñitam / sa niścayena yoktavyo yogo 'nirviṇṇacetasā /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 23: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃkalpaprabhavān kāmāṃs tyaktvā sarvān aśeṣataḥ / manasaivendriyagrāmaṃ viniyamya samantataḥ /
verses.translation: One should gradually practise this yoga under the guidance of a bona fide spiritual master. Giving up all the objects of sense desire, and controlling the senses with the mind.

verses.transliteration: śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛtigṛhītayā / ātmasaṃsthaṃ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiṃ cid api cintayet /
verses.translation: Gradually, step by step, one should become situated in trance by means of intelligence sustained by full conviction, and thus the mind should be fixed on the self alone and should think of nothing else.

verses.transliteration: yato yato niścarati manaś cañcalam asthiram / tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmany eva vaśaṃ nayet /
verses.translation: From wherever the mind wanders due to its flickering and unsteady nature, one must certainly withdraw it and bring it back under the control of the self.

verses.transliteration: praśāntamanasaṃ hy enaṃ yoginaṃ sukham uttamam / upaiti śāntarajasaṃ brahmabhūtam akalmaṣam /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 27: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yuñjann evaṃ sadātmānaṃ yogī vigatakalmaṣaḥ / sukhena brahmasaṃsparśam atyantaṃ sukham aśnute /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 28: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvabhūtastham ātmānaṃ sarvabhūtāni cātmani / īkṣate yogayuktātmā sarvatra samadarśanaḥ /
verses.translation: A true yogi observes Me in all beings and also sees every being in Me. Indeed, the self-realised person sees Me, the same Supreme Lord, everywhere.

verses.transliteration: yo māṃ paśyati sarvatra sarvaṃ ca mayi paśyati / tasyāhaṃ na praṇaśyāmi sa ca me na praṇaśyati /
verses.translation: For one who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, I am never lost, nor is he ever lost to Me.

verses.transliteration: sarvabhūtasthitaṃ yo māṃ bhajaty ekatvam āsthitaḥ / sarvathā vartamāno 'pi sa yogī mayi vartate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 31: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṃ paśyati yo 'rjuna / sukhaṃ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṃ sa yogī paramo mataḥ /
verses.translation: He is a perfect yogi who, by comparison to his own self, sees the true equality of all beings, in both their happiness and their distress, O Arjuna.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / yo 'yaṃ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana / etasyāhaṃ na paśyāmi cañcalatvāt sthitiṃ sthirām /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 33: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: cañcalaṃ hi manaḥ kṛṣṇa pramāthi balavad dṛḍham / tasyāhaṃ nigrahaṃ manye vāyor iva suduṣkaram /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: O Krishna, the mind is restless, turbulent, obstinate, and very strong, and it seems to me that it is more difficult to control than the wind.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / asaṃśayaṃ mahābāho mano durṇigrahaṃ calam / abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa ca gṛhyate /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.

verses.transliteration: asaṃyatātmanā yogo duṣprāpa iti me matiḥ / vaśyātmanā tu yatatā śakyo 'vāptum upāyataḥ /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 36: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / ayatiḥ śraddhayopeto yogāc calitamānasaḥ / aprāpya yogasaṃsiddhiṃ kāṃ gatiṃ kṛṣṇa gacchati /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 37: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kac cin nobhayavibhraṣṭaś chinnābhram iva naśyati / apratiṣṭho mahābāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 38: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: etan me saṃśayaṃ kṛṣṇa chettum arhasy aśeṣataḥ / tvadanyaḥ saṃśayasyāsya chettā na hy upapadyate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 39: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśas tasya vidyate / na hi kalyāṇakṛt kaś cid durgatiṃ tāta gacchati /
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Son of Pritha, a transcendentalist engaged in auspicious activities does not meet with destruction either in this world or in the spiritual world; one who does good, My friend, is never overcome by evil.

verses.transliteration: prāpya puṇyakṛtāṃ lokān uṣitvā śāśvatīḥ samāḥ / śucīnāṃ śrīmatāṃ gehe yogabhraṣṭo 'bhijāyate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 41: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: atha vā yoginām eva kule bhavati dhīmatām / etad dhi durlabhataraṃ loke janma yad īdṛśam /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 42: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tatra taṃ buddhisaṃyogaṃ labhate paurvadehikam / yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ saṃsiddhau kurunandana /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 43: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pūrvābhyāsena tenaiva hriyate hy avaśo 'pi saḥ / jijñāsur api yogasya śabdabrahmātivartate /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 44: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prayatnād yatamānas tu yogī saṃśuddhakilbiṣaḥ / anekajanmasaṃsiddhas tato yāti parāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 45: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tapasvibhyo 'dhiko yogī jñānibhyo 'pi mato 'dhikaḥ / karmibhyaś cādhiko yogī tasmād yogī bhavārjuna /
verses.translation: In the Dhyana Yoga (Chapter 6), verse 46: Krishna describes the practice of meditation, the control of mind, and the characteristics of the yogi who has achieved union with the supreme. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yoginām api sarveṣāṃ madgatenāntarātmanā / śraddhāvān bhajate yo māṃ sa me yuktatamo mataḥ /
verses.translation: And of all yogis, the one with great faith who always abides in Me, thinks of Me within himself, and renders transcendental loving service to Me — he is the most intimately united with Me in yoga and is the highest of all.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / mayy āsaktamanāḥ pārtha yogaṃ yuñjan madāśrayaḥ / asaṃśayaṃ samagraṃ māṃ yathā jñāsyasi tac chṛṇu /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Now hear, O son of Pritha, how by practising yoga in full consciousness of Me, with mind attached to Me, you can know Me in full, free from doubt.

verses.transliteration: jñānaṃ te 'haṃ savijñānam idaṃ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ / yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo 'nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 2: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṃ sahasreṣu kaś cid yatati siddhaye / yatatām api siddhānāṃ kaś cin māṃ vetti tattvataḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 3: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhūmir āpo 'nalo vāyuḥ khaṃ mano buddhir eva ca / ahaṃkāra itīyaṃ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā /
verses.translation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence, and false ego — all together these eight constitute My separated material energies.

verses.transliteration: apareyam itas tv anyāṃ prakṛtiṃ viddhi me parām / jīvabhūtāṃ mahābāho yayedaṃ dhāryate jagat /
verses.translation: Besides these, O mighty-armed Arjuna, there is another, superior energy of Mine, which comprises the living entities who are exploiting the resources of this material, inferior nature.

verses.transliteration: etadyonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty upadhāraya / ahaṃ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 6: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mattaḥ parataraṃ nānyat kiṃ cid asti dhanaṃjaya / mayi sarvam idaṃ protaṃ sūtre maṇigaṇā iva /
verses.translation: O conqueror of wealth, there is no truth superior to Me. Everything rests upon Me, as pearls are strung on a thread.

verses.transliteration: raso 'ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśisūryayoḥ / praṇavaḥ sarvavedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṃ nṛṣu /
verses.translation: O son of Kunti, I am the taste of water, the light of the sun and the moon, the syllable om in the Vedic mantras; I am the sound in ether and ability in man.

verses.transliteration: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṃ ca tejaś cāsmi vibhāvasau / jīvanaṃ sarvabhūteṣu tapaś cāsmi tapasviṣu /
verses.translation: I am the original fragrance of the earth, and I am the heat in fire. I am the life of all that lives, and I am the penances of all ascetics.

verses.transliteration: bījaṃ māṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ viddhi pārtha sanātanam / buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham /
verses.translation: O son of Pritha, know that I am the original seed of all existences, the intelligence of the intelligent, and the prowess of all powerful men.

verses.transliteration: balaṃ balavatāṃ cāhaṃ kāmarāgavivarjitam / dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo 'smi bharatarṣabha /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 11: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ye caiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāś ca ye / matta eveti tān viddhi na tv ahaṃ teṣu te mayi /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 12: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tribhir guṇamayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṃ jagat / mohitaṃ nābhijānāti mām ebhyaḥ param avyayam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 13: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: daivī hy eṣā guṇamayī mama māyā duratyayā / mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṃ taranti te /
verses.translation: This divine energy of Mine, consisting of the three modes of material nature, is difficult to overcome. But those who have surrendered unto Me can easily cross beyond it.

verses.transliteration: na māṃ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ / māyayāpahṛtajñānā āsuraṃ bhāvam āśritāḥ /
verses.translation: Those miscreants who are grossly foolish, who are lowest among mankind, whose knowledge is stolen by illusion, and who partake of the atheistic nature of demons do not surrender unto Me.

verses.transliteration: caturvidhā bhajante māṃ janāḥ sukṛtino 'rjuna / ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha /
verses.translation: O best among the Bharatas, four kinds of pious men begin to render devotional service unto Me — the distressed, the desirer of wealth, the inquisitive, and he who is searching for knowledge of the Absolute.

verses.transliteration: teṣāṃ jñānī nityayukta ekabhaktir viśiṣyate / priyo hi jñānino 'tyartham ahaṃ sa ca mama priyaḥ /
verses.translation: Of these, the one who is in full knowledge and who is always engaged in pure devotional service is the best. For I am very dear to him, and he is dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tv ātmaiva me matam / āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 18: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bahūnāṃ janmanām ante jñānavān māṃ prapadyate / vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ /
verses.translation: After many births and deaths, he who is actually in knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be the cause of all causes and all that is. Such a great soul is very rare.

verses.transliteration: kāmais tais tair hṛtajñānāḥ prapadyante 'nyadevatāḥ / taṃ taṃ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 20: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yo yo yāṃ yāṃ tanuṃ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārcitum icchati / tasya tasyācalāṃ śraddhāṃ tām eva vidadhāmy aham /
verses.translation: I am in everyone's heart as the Supersoul. As soon as one desires to worship the demigods, I make his faith steady so that he can devote himself to some particular deity.

verses.transliteration: sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyā rādhanam īhate / labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 22: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: antavat tu phalaṃ teṣāṃ tad bhavaty alpamedhasām / devān devayajo yānti madbhaktā yānti mām api /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 23: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: avyaktaṃ vyaktim āpannaṃ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ / paraṃ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 24: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yogamāyāsamāvṛtaḥ / mūḍho 'yaṃ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 25: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vedāhaṃ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna / bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṃ tu veda na kaś cana /
verses.translation: O Arjuna, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, I know everything that has happened in the past, all that is happening in the present, and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities; but Me no one knows.

verses.transliteration: icchādveṣasamutthena dvandvamohena bhārata / sarvabhūtāni saṃmohaṃ sarge yānti paraṃtapa /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 27: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yeṣāṃ tv antagataṃ pāpaṃ janānāṃ puṇyakarmaṇām / te dvandvamohanirmuktā bhajante māṃ dṛḍhavratāḥ /
verses.translation: Persons who have acted piously in previous lives and in this life and whose sinful actions are completely eradicated are freed from the dualities of delusion, and they engage themselves in My service with determination.

verses.transliteration: jarāmaraṇamokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye / te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṃ karma cākhilam /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 29: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṃ māṃ sādhiyajñaṃ ca ye viduḥ / prayāṇakāle 'pi ca māṃ te vidur yuktacetasaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Jnana Vijnana Yoga (Chapter 7), verse 30: Krishna reveals his divine nature as the ground of all existence. He describes two aspects of his power — the higher spiritual and the lower material. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / kiṃ tad brahma kim adhyātmaṃ kiṃ karma puruṣottama / adhibhūtaṃ ca kiṃ proktam adhidaivaṃ kim ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 1: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: adhiyajñaḥ kathaṃ ko 'tra dehe 'smin madhusūdana / prayāṇakāle ca kathaṃ jñeyo 'si niyatātmabhiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 2: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / akṣaraṃ brahma paramaṃ svabhāvo 'dhyātmam ucyate / bhūtabhāvodbhavakaro visargaḥ karmasaṃjñitaḥ /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: The indestructible, transcendental living entity is called Brahman, and its eternal nature is called adhyatma, the self. Action pertaining to the development of the material bodies of the living entities is called karma, or fruitive activities.

verses.transliteration: adhibhūtaṃ kṣaro bhāvaḥ puruṣaś cādhidaivatam / adhiyajño 'ham evātra dehe dehabhṛtāṃ vara /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 4: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: antakāle ca mām eva smaran muktvā kalevaram / yaḥ prayāti sa madbhāvaṃ yāti nāsty atra saṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: And whoever, at the time of death, gives up the body remembering Me alone, reaches My state. Of this there is no doubt.

verses.transliteration: yaṃ yaṃ vāpi smaran bhāvaṃ tyajaty ante kalevaram / taṃ tam evaiti kaunteya sadā tadbhāvabhāvitaḥ /
verses.translation: Whatever state of being one remembers when he quits his body, O son of Kunti, that state he will attain without fail.

verses.transliteration: tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu mām anusmara yudhya ca / mayy arpitamanobuddhir mām evaiṣyasy asaṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: Therefore, Arjuna, you should always think of Me in the form of Krishna and at the same time carry out your prescribed duty of fighting. With your activities dedicated to Me and your mind and intelligence fixed on Me, you will attain Me without doubt.

verses.transliteration: abhyāsayogayuktena cetasā nānyagāminā / paramaṃ puruṣaṃ divyaṃ yāti pārthānucintayan /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 8: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kaviṃ purāṇam anuśāsitāram; aṇor aṇīyāṃsam anusmared yaḥ / sarvasya dhātāram acintyarūpam; ādityavarṇaṃ tamasaḥ parastāt /
verses.translation: One should meditate upon the Supreme Person as the one who knows everything, as He who is the oldest, who is the controller, who is smaller than the smallest, who is the maintainer of everything, who is beyond all material conception, who is inconceivable, and who is always a person.

verses.transliteration: prayāṇakāle manasācalena; bhaktyā yukto yogabalena caiva / bhruvor madhye prāṇam āveśya samyak; sa taṃ paraṃ puruṣam upaiti divyam /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 10: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yad akṣaraṃ vedavido vadanti; viśanti yad yatayo vītarāgāḥ / yad icchanto brahmacaryaṃ caranti; tat te padaṃ saṃgraheṇa pravakṣye /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 11: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvadvārāṇi saṃyamya mano hṛdi nirudhya ca / mūrdhny ādhāyātmanaḥ prāṇam āsthito yogadhāraṇām /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 12: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: om ity ekākṣaraṃ brahma vyāharan mām anusmaran / yaḥ prayāti tyajan dehaṃ sa yāti paramāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: After being situated in this yoga practice and vibrating the sacred syllable om, the supreme combination of letters, if one thinks of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and quits his body, he will certainly reach the spiritual planets.

verses.transliteration: ananyacetāḥ satataṃ yo māṃ smarati nityaśaḥ / tasyāhaṃ sulabhaḥ pārtha nityayuktasya yoginaḥ /
verses.translation: For one who always remembers Me without deviation, I am easy to obtain, O son of Pritha, because of his constant engagement in devotional service.

verses.transliteration: mām upetya punarjanma duḥkhālayam aśāśvatam / nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ saṃsiddhiṃ paramāṃ gatāḥ /
verses.translation: After attaining Me, the great souls, who are yogis in devotion, never return to this temporary world, which is full of miseries, because they have attained the highest perfection.

verses.transliteration: ā brahmabhuvanāl lokāḥ punarāvartino 'rjuna / mām upetya tu kaunteya punarjanma na vidyate /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 16: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sahasrayugaparyantam ahar yad brahmaṇo viduḥ / rātriṃ yugasahasrāntāṃ te 'horātravido janāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 17: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: avyaktād vyaktayaḥ sarvāḥ prabhavanty aharāgame / rātryāgame pralīyante tatraivāvyaktasaṃjñake /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 18: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhūtagrāmaḥ sa evāyaṃ bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate / rātryāgame 'vaśaḥ pārtha prabhavaty aharāgame /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 19: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: paras tasmāt tu bhāvo 'nyo 'vyakto 'vyaktāt sanātanaḥ / yaḥ sa sarveṣu bhūteṣu naśyatsu na vinaśyati /
verses.translation: Yet there is another unmanifest nature, which is eternal and is transcendental to this manifested and unmanifested matter. It is supreme and is never annihilated. When all in this world is annihilated, that part remains as it is.

verses.transliteration: avyakto 'kṣara ity uktas tam āhuḥ paramāṃ gatim / yaṃ prāpya na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṃ mama /
verses.translation: That which the Vedantists describe as unmanifest and infallible, that which is known as the supreme destination, that place from which, having attained it, one never returns — that is My supreme abode.

verses.transliteration: puruṣaḥ sa paraḥ pārtha bhaktyā labhyas tv ananyayā / yasyāntaḥsthāni bhūtāni yena sarvam idaṃ tatam /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 22: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yatra kāle tv anāvṛttim āvṛttiṃ caiva yoginaḥ / prayātā yānti taṃ kālaṃ vakṣyāmi bharatarṣabha /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 23: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: agnir jyotir ahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇmāsā uttarāyaṇam / tatra prayātā gacchanti brahma brahmavido janāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 24: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dhūmo rātris tathā kṛṣṇaḥ ṣaṇmāsā dakṣiṇāyanam / tatra cāndramasaṃ jyotir yogī prāpya nivartate /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 25: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śuklakṛṣṇe gatī hy ete jagataḥ śāśvate mate / ekayā yāty anāvṛttim anyayāvartate punaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 26: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: naite sṛtī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaś cana / tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu yogayukto bhavārjuna /
verses.translation: In the Akshara Brahma Yoga (Chapter 8), verse 27: Krishna explains Brahman, karma, and the cosmic cycles of creation. He describes what the soul experiences at the moment of death and the paths to liberation. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vedeṣu yajñeṣu tapaḥsu caiva; dāneṣu yat puṇyaphalaṃ pradiṣṭam / atyeti tat sarvam idaṃ viditvā; yogī paraṃ sthānam upaiti cādyam /
verses.translation: A person who accepts the path of devotional service is not bereft of the results derived from studying the Vedas, performing austere sacrifices, giving charity, or pursuing philosophical and fruitive activities. Simply by performing devotional service, he attains all these, and at the end he reaches the supreme eternal abode.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / idaṃ tu te guhyatamaṃ pravakṣyāmy anasūyave / jñānaṃ vijñānasahitaṃ yaj jñātvā mokṣyase 'śubhāt /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: My dear Arjuna, because you are never envious of Me, I shall impart to you this most confidential knowledge and realisation, knowing which you shall be relieved of the miseries of material existence.

verses.transliteration: rājavidyā rājaguhyaṃ pavitram idam uttamam / pratyakṣāvagamaṃ dharmyaṃ susukhaṃ kartum avyayam /
verses.translation: This knowledge is the king of education, the most secret of all secrets. It is the purest knowledge, and because it gives direct perception of the self by realisation, it is the perfection of religion. It is everlasting, and it is joyfully performed.

verses.transliteration: aśraddadhānāḥ puruṣā dharmasyāsya paraṃtapa / aprāpya māṃ nivartante mṛtyusaṃsāravartmani /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 3: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mayā tatam idaṃ sarvaṃ jagad avyaktamūrtinā / matsthāni sarvabhūtāni na cāhaṃ teṣv avasthitaḥ /
verses.translation: By Me, in My unmanifested form, this entire universe is pervaded. All beings are in Me, but I am not in them.

verses.transliteration: na ca matsthāni bhūtāni paśya me yogam aiśvaram / bhūtabhṛn na ca bhūtastho mamātmā bhūtabhāvanaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 5: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathākāśasthito nityaṃ vāyuḥ sarvatrago mahān / tathā sarvāṇi bhūtāni matsthānīty upadhāraya /
verses.translation: Understand that as the mighty wind, blowing everywhere, rests always in the sky, all created beings rest in Me.

verses.transliteration: sarvabhūtāni kaunteya prakṛtiṃ yānti māmikām / kalpakṣaye punas tāni kalpādau visṛjāmy aham /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 7: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prakṛtiṃ svām avaṣṭabhya visṛjāmi punaḥ punaḥ / bhūtagrāmam imaṃ kṛtsnam avaśaṃ prakṛter vaśāt /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 8: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na ca māṃ tāni karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanaṃjaya / udāsīnavad āsīnam asaktaṃ teṣu karmasu /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 9: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mayādhyakṣeṇa prakṛtiḥ sūyate sacarācaram / hetunānena kaunteya jagad viparivartate /
verses.translation: This material nature, which is one of My energies, is working under My direction, O son of Kunti, producing all moving and nonmoving beings. Under its rule this manifestation is created and annihilated again and again.

verses.transliteration: avajānanti māṃ mūḍhā mānuṣīṃ tanum āśritam / paraṃ bhāvam ajānanto mama bhūtamaheśvaram /
verses.translation: Fools deride Me when I descend in the human form. They do not know My transcendental nature as the Supreme Lord of all that be.

verses.transliteration: moghāśā moghakarmāṇo moghajñānā vicetasaḥ / rākṣasīm āsurīṃ caiva prakṛtiṃ mohinīṃ śritāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 12: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mahātmānas tu māṃ pārtha daivīṃ prakṛtim āśritāḥ / bhajanty ananyamanaso jñātvā bhūtādim avyayam /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 13: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: satataṃ kīrtayanto māṃ yatantaś ca dṛḍhavratāḥ / namasyantaś ca māṃ bhaktyā nityayuktā upāsate /
verses.translation: Always chanting My glories, endeavouring with great determination, bowing down before Me, these great souls perpetually worship Me with devotion.

verses.transliteration: jñānayajñena cāpy anye yajanto mām upāsate / ekatvena pṛthaktvena bahudhā viśvatomukham /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 15: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃ kratur ahaṃ yajñaḥ svadhāham aham auṣadham / mantro 'ham aham evājyam aham agnir ahaṃ hutam /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 16: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pitāham asya jagato mātā dhātā pitāmahaḥ / vedyaṃ pavitram oṃkāra ṛk sāma yajur eva ca /
verses.translation: I am the father of this universe, the mother, the support, and the grandsire. I am the object of knowledge, the purifier, and the syllable om. I am also the Rig, the Sama, and the Yajur Vedas.

verses.transliteration: gatir bhartā prabhuḥ sākṣī nivāsaḥ śaraṇaṃ suhṛt / prabhavaḥ pralayaḥ sthānaṃ nidhānaṃ bījam avyayam /
verses.translation: I am the goal, the sustainer, the master, the witness, the abode, the refuge, and the most dear friend. I am the creation and the annihilation, the basis of everything, the resting place, and the eternal seed.

verses.transliteration: tapāmy aham ahaṃ varṣaṃ nigṛhṇāmy utsṛjāmi ca / amṛtaṃ caiva mṛtyuś ca sad asac cāham arjuna /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 19: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: traividyā māṃ somapāḥ pūtapāpā; yajñair iṣṭvā svargatiṃ prārthayante / te puṇyam āsādya surendralokam; aśnanti divyān divi devabhogān /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 20: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: te taṃ bhuktvā svargalokaṃ viśālaṃ; kṣīṇe puṇye martyalokaṃ viśanti / evaṃ trayīdharmam anuprapannā; gatāgataṃ kāmakāmā labhante /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 21: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ananyāś cintayanto māṃ ye janāḥ paryupāsate / teṣāṃ nityābhiyuktānāṃ yogakṣemaṃ vahāmy aham /
verses.translation: But those who always worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — to them I carry what they lack, and I preserve what they have.

verses.transliteration: ye 'py anyadevatābhaktā yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ / te 'pi mām eva kaunteya yajanty avidhipūrvakam /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 23: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃ hi sarvayajñānāṃ bhoktā ca prabhur eva ca / na tu mām abhijānanti tattvenātaś cyavanti te /
verses.translation: In the Raja Vidya Raja Guhya Yoga (Chapter 9), verse 24: The most secret knowledge: Krishna reveals his supreme nature and his unconditional love for all beings. He accepts even the simplest offering given with devotion. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yānti devavratā devān pitṝn yānti pitṛvratāḥ / bhūtāni yānti bhūtejyā yānti madyājino 'pi mām /
verses.translation: Those who worship the demigods will take birth among the demigods; those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors; those who worship ghosts and spirits will take birth among such beings; and those who worship Me will live with Me.

verses.transliteration: patraṃ puṣpaṃ phalaṃ toyaṃ yo me bhaktyā prayacchati / tad ahaṃ bhaktyupahṛtam aśnāmi prayatātmanaḥ /
verses.translation: If one offers Me with love and devotion a leaf, a flower, fruit, or water, I will accept it.

verses.transliteration: yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat / yat tapasyasi kaunteya tat kuruṣva madarpaṇam /
verses.translation: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform — do that, O son of Kunti, as an offering to Me.

verses.transliteration: śubhāśubhaphalair evaṃ mokṣyase karmabandhanaiḥ / saṃnyāsayogayuktātmā vimukto mām upaiṣyasi /
verses.translation: In this way you will be freed from bondage to work and its auspicious and inauspicious results. With your mind fixed on Me in this principle of renunciation, you will be liberated and come to Me.

verses.transliteration: samo 'haṃ sarvabhūteṣu na me dveṣyo 'sti na priyaḥ / ye bhajanti tu māṃ bhaktyā mayi te teṣu cāpy aham /
verses.translation: I envy no one, nor am I partial to anyone. I am equal to all. But whoever renders service unto Me in devotion is a friend, is in Me, and I am also a friend to him.

verses.transliteration: api cet sudurācāro bhajate mām ananyabhāk / sādhur eva sa mantavyaḥ samyag vyavasito hi saḥ /
verses.translation: Even if one commits the most abominable action, if he is engaged in devotional service he is to be considered saintly because he is properly situated in his determination.

verses.transliteration: kṣipraṃ bhavati dharmātmā śaśvacchāntiṃ nigacchati / kaunteya pratijānīhi na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśyati /
verses.translation: He quickly becomes righteous and attains lasting peace. O son of Kunti, declare it boldly that My devotee never perishes.

verses.transliteration: māṃ hi pārtha vyapāśritya ye 'pi syuḥ pāpayonayaḥ / striyo vaiśyās tathā śūdrās te 'pi yānti parāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: O son of Pritha, those who take shelter in Me, though they be of lower birth — women, vaishyas (merchants), as well as shudras (workers) — can attain the supreme destination.

verses.transliteration: kiṃ punar brāhmaṇāḥ puṇyā bhaktā rājarṣayas tathā / anityam asukhaṃ lokam imaṃ prāpya bhajasva mām /
verses.translation: How much more this is so of the righteous brahmanas, the devotees, and the saintly kings. Therefore, having come to this temporary, miserable world, engage in loving service unto Me.

verses.transliteration: manmanā bhava madbhakto madyājī māṃ namaskuru / mām evaiṣyasi yuktvaivam ātmānaṃ matparāyaṇaḥ /
verses.translation: Engage your mind always in thinking of Me, become My devotee, offer obeisances to Me, and worship Me. Being completely absorbed in Me, surely you will come to Me.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / bhūya eva mahābāho śṛṇu me paramaṃ vacaḥ / yat te 'haṃ prīyamāṇāya vakṣyāmi hitakāmyayā /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: My dear friend, mighty-armed Arjuna, listen again to My supreme word, which I shall impart to you for your benefit and which will give you great joy.

verses.transliteration: na me viduḥ suragaṇāḥ prabhavaṃ na maharṣayaḥ / aham ādir hi devānāṃ maharṣīṇāṃ ca sarvaśaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 2: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yo mām ajam anādiṃ ca vetti lokamaheśvaram / asaṃmūḍhaḥ sa martyeṣu sarvapāpaiḥ pramucyate /
verses.translation: He who knows Me as the unborn, as the beginningless, as the Supreme Lord of all the worlds — he only, undeluded among men, is freed from all sins.

verses.transliteration: buddhir jñānam asaṃmohaḥ kṣamā satyaṃ damaḥ śamaḥ / sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ bhavo 'bhāvo bhayaṃ cābhayam eva ca /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 4: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahiṃsā samatā tuṣṭis tapo dānaṃ yaśo 'yaśaḥ / bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṃ matta eva pṛthagvidhāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 5: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: maharṣayaḥ sapta pūrve catvāro manavas tathā / madbhāvā mānasā jātā yeṣāṃ loka imāḥ prajāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 6: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: etāṃ vibhūtiṃ yogaṃ ca mama yo vetti tattvataḥ / so 'vikampena yogena yujyate nātra saṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 7: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃ sarvasya prabhavo mattaḥ sarvaṃ pravartate / iti matvā bhajante māṃ budhā bhāvasamanvitāḥ /
verses.translation: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service and worship Me with all their hearts.

verses.transliteration: maccittā madgataprāṇā bodhayantaḥ parasparam / kathayantaś ca māṃ nityaṃ tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca /
verses.translation: The thoughts of My pure devotees dwell in Me, their lives are fully devoted to My service, and they derive great satisfaction and bliss from always enlightening one another and conversing about Me.

verses.transliteration: teṣāṃ satatayuktānāṃ bhajatāṃ prītipūrvakam / dadāmi buddhiyogaṃ taṃ yena mām upayānti te /
verses.translation: To those who are constantly devoted to serving Me with love, I give the understanding by which they can come to Me.

verses.transliteration: teṣām evānukampārtham aham ajñānajaṃ tamaḥ / nāśayāmy ātmabhāvastho jñānadīpena bhāsvatā /
verses.translation: To show them special mercy, I, dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge the darkness born of ignorance.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / paraṃ brahma paraṃ dhāma pavitraṃ paramaṃ bhavān / puruṣaṃ śāśvataṃ divyam ādidevam ajaṃ vibhum /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 12: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āhus tvām ṛṣayaḥ sarve devarṣir nāradas tathā / asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṃ caiva bravīṣi me /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 13: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvam etad ṛtaṃ manye yan māṃ vadasi keśava / na hi te bhagavan vyaktiṃ vidur devā na dānavāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 14: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: svayam evātmanātmānaṃ vettha tvaṃ puruṣottama / bhūtabhāvana bhūteśa devadeva jagatpate /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 15: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vaktum arhasy aśeṣeṇa divyā hy ātmavibhūtayaḥ / yābhir vibhūtibhir lokān imāṃs tvaṃ vyāpya tiṣṭhasi /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 16: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kathaṃ vidyām ahaṃ yogiṃs tvāṃ sadā paricintayan / keṣu keṣu ca bhāveṣu cintyo 'si bhagavan mayā /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 17: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vistareṇātmano yogaṃ vibhūtiṃ ca janārdana / bhūyaḥ kathaya tṛptir hi śṛṇvato nāsti me 'mṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 18: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / hanta te kathayiṣyāmi divyā hy ātmavibhūtayaḥ / prādhānyataḥ kuruśreṣṭha nāsty anto vistarasya me /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 19: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aham ātmā guḍākeśa sarvabhūtāśayasthitaḥ / aham ādiś ca madhyaṃ ca bhūtānām anta eva ca /
verses.translation: I am the Supersoul, O Arjuna, seated in the hearts of all living entities. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

verses.transliteration: ādityānām ahaṃ viṣṇur jyotiṣāṃ ravir aṃśumān / marīcir marutām asmi nakṣatrāṇām ahaṃ śaśī /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 21: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vedānāṃ sāmavedo 'smi devānām asmi vāsavaḥ / indriyāṇāṃ manaś cāsmi bhūtānām asmi cetanā /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 22: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: rudrāṇāṃ śaṃkaraś cāsmi vitteśo yakṣarakṣasām / vasūnāṃ pāvakaś cāsmi meruḥ śikhariṇām aham /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 23: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: purodhasāṃ ca mukhyaṃ māṃ viddhi pārtha bṛhaspatim / senānīnām ahaṃ skandaḥ sarasām asmi sāgaraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 24: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: maharṣīṇāṃ bhṛgur ahaṃ girām asmy ekam akṣaram / yajñānāṃ japayajño 'smi sthāvarāṇāṃ himālayaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 25: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aśvatthaḥ sarvavṛkṣāṇāṃ devarṣīṇāṃ ca nāradaḥ / gandharvāṇāṃ citrarathaḥ siddhānāṃ kapilo muniḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 26: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: uccaiḥśravasam aśvānāṃ viddhi mām amṛtodbhavam / airāvataṃ gajendrāṇāṃ narāṇāṃ ca narādhipam /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 27: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āyudhānām ahaṃ vajraṃ dhenūnām asmi kāmadhuk / prajanaś cāsmi kandarpaḥ sarpāṇām asmi vāsukiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 28: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anantaś cāsmi nāgānāṃ varuṇo yādasām aham / pitṝṇām aryamā cāsmi yamaḥ saṃyamatām aham /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 29: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: prahlādaś cāsmi daityānāṃ kālaḥ kalayatām aham / mṛgāṇāṃ ca mṛgendro 'haṃ vainateyaś ca pakṣiṇām /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 30: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pavanaḥ pavatām asmi rāmaḥ śastrabhṛtām aham / jhaṣāṇāṃ makaraś cāsmi srotasām asmi jāhnavī /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 31: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sargāṇām ādir antaś ca madhyaṃ caivāham arjuna / adhyātmavidyā vidyānāṃ vādaḥ pravadatām aham /
verses.translation: Of all creations I am the beginning and the end and also the middle, O Arjuna. Of all the sciences I am the spiritual science of the self, and among logicians I am the conclusive truth.

verses.transliteration: akṣarāṇām akāro 'smi dvandvaḥ sāmāsikasya ca / aham evākṣayaḥ kālo dhātāhaṃ viśvatomukhaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 33: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mṛtyuḥ sarvaharaś cāham udbhavaś ca bhaviṣyatām / kīrtiḥ śrīr vāk ca nārīṇāṃ smṛtir medhā dhṛtiḥ kṣamā /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 34: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bṛhatsāma tathā sāmnāṃ gāyatrī chandasām aham / māsānāṃ mārgaśīrṣo 'ham ṛtūnāṃ kusumākaraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 35: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dyūtaṃ chalayatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham / jayo 'smi vyavasāyo 'smi sattvaṃ sattvavatām aham /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 36: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vṛṣṇīnāṃ vāsudevo 'smi pāṇḍavānāṃ dhanaṃjayaḥ / munīnām apy ahaṃ vyāsaḥ kavīnām uśanā kaviḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 37: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: daṇḍo damayatām asmi nītir asmi jigīṣatām / maunaṃ caivāsmi guhyānāṃ jñānaṃ jñānavatām aham /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 38: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yac cāpi sarvabhūtānāṃ bījaṃ tad aham arjuna / na tad asti vinā yat syān mayā bhūtaṃ carācaram /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 39: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nānto 'sti mama divyānāṃ vibhūtīnāṃ paraṃtapa / eṣa tūddeśataḥ prokto vibhūter vistaro mayā /
verses.translation: In the Vibhuti Yoga (Chapter 10), verse 40: Krishna describes his infinite divine manifestations — he is the best of every category of being. Knowing his glory, one can love and worship him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yad yad vibhūtimat sattvaṃ śrīmad ūrjitam eva vā / tat tad evāvagaccha tvaṃ mama tejo 'ṃśasaṃbhavam /
verses.translation: Know that all opulent, beautiful, and glorious creations spring from but a spark of My splendour.

verses.transliteration: atha vā bahunaitena kiṃ jñātena tavārjuna / viṣṭabhyāham idaṃ kṛtsnam ekāṃśena sthito jagat /
verses.translation: But what need is there, Arjuna, for all this detailed knowledge? With a single fragment of Myself I pervade and support this entire universe.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / madanugrahāya paramaṃ guhyam adhyātmasaṃjñitam / yat tvayoktaṃ vacas tena moho 'yaṃ vigato mama /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 1: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhavāpyayau hi bhūtānāṃ śrutau vistaraśo mayā / tvattaḥ kamalapatrākṣa māhātmyam api cāvyayam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 2: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: evam etad yathāttha tvam ātmānaṃ parameśvara / draṣṭum icchāmi te rūpam aiśvaraṃ puruṣottama /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 3: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: manyase yadi tac chakyaṃ mayā draṣṭum iti prabho / yogeśvara tato me tvaṃ darśayātmānam avyayam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 4: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / paśya me pārtha rūpāṇi śataśo 'tha sahasraśaḥ / nānāvidhāni divyāni nānāvarṇākṛtīni ca /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: My dear Arjuna, O son of Pritha, see now My opulences, hundreds of thousands of varied divine and multicoloured forms.

verses.transliteration: paśyādityān vasūn rudrān aśvinau marutas tathā / bahūny adṛṣṭapūrvāṇi paśyāścaryāṇi bhārata /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 6: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ihaikasthaṃ jagat kṛtsnaṃ paśyādya sacarācaram / mama dehe guḍākeśa yac cānyad draṣṭum icchasi /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 7: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na tu māṃ śakyase draṣṭum anenaiva svacakṣuṣā / divyaṃ dadāmi te cakṣuḥ paśya me yogam aiśvaram /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 8: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / evam uktvā tato rājan mahāyogeśvaro hariḥ / darśayām āsa pārthāya paramaṃ rūpam aiśvaram /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 9: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anekavaktranayanam anekādbhutadarśanam / anekadivyābharaṇaṃ divyānekodyatāyudham /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 10: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: divyamālyāmbaradharaṃ divyagandhānulepanam / sarvāścaryamayaṃ devam anantaṃ viśvatomukham /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 11: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: divi sūryasahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthitā / yadi bhāḥ sadṛśī sā syād bhāsas tasya mahātmanaḥ /
verses.translation: If hundreds of thousands of suns were to rise at once into the sky, their radiance might resemble the effulgence of the Supreme Person in that universal form.

verses.transliteration: tatraikasthaṃ jagat kṛtsnaṃ pravibhaktam anekadhā / apaśyad devadevasya śarīre pāṇḍavas tadā /
verses.translation: At that time Arjuna could see in the universal form of the Lord the unlimited expansions of the universe situated in one place although divided into many, many thousands.

verses.transliteration: tataḥ sa vismayāviṣṭo hṛṣṭaromā dhanaṃjayaḥ / praṇamya śirasā devaṃ kṛtāñjalir abhāṣata /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 14: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / paśyāmi devāṃs tava deva dehe; sarvāṃs tathā bhūtaviśeṣasaṃghān / brahmāṇam īśaṃ kamalāsanastham; ṛṣīṃś ca sarvān uragāṃś ca divyān /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 15: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anekabāhūdaravaktranetraṃ; paśyāmi tvā sarvato 'nantarūpam / nāntaṃ na madhyaṃ na punas tavādiṃ; paśyāmi viśveśvara viśvarūpa /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 16: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kirīṭinaṃ gadinaṃ cakriṇaṃ ca; tejorāśiṃ sarvato dīptimantam / paśyāmi tvāṃ durnirīkṣyaṃ samantād; dīptānalārkadyutim aprameyam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 17: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tvam akṣaraṃ paramaṃ veditavyaṃ; tvam asya viśvasya paraṃ nidhānam / tvam avyayaḥ śāśvatadharmagoptā; sanātanas tvaṃ puruṣo mato me /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 18: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anādimadhyāntam anantavīryam; anantabāhuṃ śaśisūryanetram / paśyāmi tvāṃ dīptahutāśavaktraṃ; svatejasā viśvam idaṃ tapantam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 19: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dyāvāpṛthivyor idam antaraṃ hi; vyāptaṃ tvayaikena diśaś ca sarvāḥ / dṛṣṭvādbhutaṃ rūpam idaṃ tavograṃ; lokatrayaṃ pravyathitaṃ mahātman /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 20: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: amī hi tvā surasaṃghā viśanti; ke cid bhītāḥ prāñjalayo gṛṇanti / svastīty uktvā maharṣisiddhasaṃghāḥ; stuvanti tvāṃ stutibhiḥ puṣkalābhiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 21: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: rudrādityā vasavo ye ca sādhyā; viśve 'śvinau marutaś coṣmapāś ca / gandharvayakṣāsurasiddhasaṃghā; vīkṣante tvāṃ vismitāś caiva sarve /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 22: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: rūpaṃ mahat te bahuvaktranetraṃ; mahābāho bahubāhūrupādam / bahūdaraṃ bahudaṃṣṭrākarālaṃ; dṛṣṭvā lokāḥ pravyathitās tathāham /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 23: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nabhaḥspṛśaṃ dīptam anekavarṇaṃ; vyāttānanaṃ dīptaviśālanetram / dṛṣṭvā hi tvāṃ pravyathitāntarātmā; dhṛtiṃ na vindāmi śamaṃ ca viṣṇo /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 24: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.
