verses.transliteration: daṃṣṭrākarālāni ca te mukhāni; dṛṣṭvaiva kālānalasaṃnibhāni / diśo na jāne na labhe ca śarma; prasīda deveśa jagannivāsa /
verses.translation: O Lord of lords, O refuge of the worlds, please be gracious to me. I cannot keep my balance seeing thus Your blazing deathlike faces and awful teeth. In all directions I am bewildered.

verses.transliteration: amī ca tvāṃ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāḥ; sarve sahaivāvanipālasaṃghaiḥ / bhīṣmo droṇaḥ sūtaputras tathāsau; sahāsmadīyair api yodhamukhyaiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 26: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vaktrāṇi te tvaramāṇā viśanti; daṃṣṭrākarālāni bhayānakāni / ke cid vilagnā daśanāntareṣu; saṃdṛśyante cūrṇitair uttamāṅgaiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 27: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathā nadīnāṃ bahavo 'mbuvegāḥ; samudram evābhimukhā dravanti / tathā tavāmī naralokavīrā; viśanti vaktrāṇy abhivijvalanti /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 28: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathā pradīptaṃ jvalanaṃ pataṃgā; viśanti nāśāya samṛddhavegāḥ / tathaiva nāśāya viśanti lokās; tavāpi vaktrāṇi samṛddhavegāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 29: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: lelihyase grasamānaḥ samantāl; lokān samagrān vadanair jvaladbhiḥ / tejobhir āpūrya jagat samagraṃ; bhāsas tavogrāḥ pratapanti viṣṇo /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 30: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ākhyāhi me ko bhavān ugrarūpo; namo 'stu te devavara prasīda / vijñātum icchāmi bhavantam ādyaṃ; na hi prajānāmi tava pravṛttim /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 31: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / kālo 'smi lokakṣayakṛt pravṛddho; lokān samāhartum iha pravṛttaḥ / ṛte 'pi tvā na bhaviṣyanti sarve; ye 'vasthitāḥ pratyanīkeṣu yodhāḥ /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Time I am, the great destroyer of the worlds, and I have come here to destroy all people. With the exception of you (the Pandavas), all the soldiers here on both sides will be slain.

verses.transliteration: tasmāt tvam uttiṣṭha yaśo labhasva; jitvā śatrūn bhuṅkṣva rājyaṃ samṛddham / mayaivaite nihatāḥ pūrvam eva; nimittamātraṃ bhava savyasācin /
verses.translation: Therefore get up. Prepare to fight and win glory. Conquer your enemies and enjoy a flourishing kingdom. They are already put to death by My arrangement, and you, O Savyasachi, can be but an instrument in the fight.

verses.transliteration: droṇaṃ ca bhīṣmaṃ ca jayadrathaṃ ca; karṇaṃ tathānyān api yodhavīrān / mayā hatāṃs tvaṃ jahi mā vyathiṣṭhā; yudhyasva jetāsi raṇe sapatnān /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 34: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / etac chrutvā vacanaṃ keśavasya; kṛtāñjalir vepamānaḥ kirīṭī / namaskṛtvā bhūya evāha kṛṣṇaṃ; sagadgadaṃ bhītabhītaḥ praṇamya /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 35: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / sthāne hṛṣīkeśa tava prakīrtyā; jagat prahṛṣyaty anurajyate ca / rakṣāṃsi bhītāni diśo dravanti; sarve namasyanti ca siddhasaṃghāḥ /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: O master of the senses, the world becomes joyful upon hearing Your name, and thus everyone becomes attached to You. Although the perfected beings offer You their respectful homage, the demons are afraid, and they flee here and there. All this is rightly done.

verses.transliteration: kasmāc ca te na nameran mahātman; garīyase brahmaṇo 'py ādikartre / ananta deveśa jagannivāsa; tvam akṣaraṃ sad asat tatparaṃ yat /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 37: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tvam ādidevaḥ puruṣaḥ purāṇas; tvam asya viśvasya paraṃ nidhānam / vettāsi vedyaṃ ca paraṃ ca dhāma; tvayā tataṃ viśvam anantarūpa /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 38: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vāyur yamo 'gnir varuṇaḥ śaśāṅkaḥ; prajāpatis tvaṃ prapitāmahaś ca / namo namas te 'stu sahasrakṛtvaḥ; punaś ca bhūyo 'pi namo namas te /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 39: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: namaḥ purastād atha pṛṣṭhatas te; namo 'stu te sarvata eva sarva / anantavīryāmitavikramas tvaṃ; sarvaṃ samāpnoṣi tato 'si sarvaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 40: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sakheti matvā prasabhaṃ yad uktaṃ; he kṛṣṇa he yādava he sakheti / ajānatā mahimānaṃ tavedaṃ; mayā pramādāt praṇayena vāpi /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 41: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yac cāvahāsārtham asatkṛto 'si; vihāraśayyāsanabhojaneṣu / eko 'tha vāpy acyuta tatsamakṣaṃ; tat kṣāmaye tvām aham aprameyam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 42: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pitāsi lokasya carācarasya; tvam asya pūjyaś ca gurur garīyān / na tvatsamo 'sty abhyadhikaḥ kuto 'nyo; lokatraye 'py apratimaprabhāva /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 43: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tasmāt praṇamya praṇidhāya kāyaṃ; prasādaye tvām aham īśam īḍyam / piteva putrasya sakheva sakhyuḥ; priyaḥ priyāyārhasi deva soḍhum /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 44: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvaṃ hṛṣito 'smi dṛṣṭvā; bhayena ca pravyathitaṃ mano me / tad eva me darśaya deva rūpaṃ; prasīda deveśa jagannivāsa /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 45: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kirīṭinaṃ gadinaṃ cakrahastam; icchāmi tvāṃ draṣṭum ahaṃ tathaiva / tenaiva rūpeṇa caturbhujena; sahasrabāho bhava viśvamūrte /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 46: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / mayā prasannena tavārjunedaṃ; rūpaṃ paraṃ darśitam ātmayogāt / tejomayaṃ viśvam anantam; ādyaṃ yan me tvadanyena na dṛṣṭapūrvam /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 47: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na veda yajñādhyayanair na dānair; na ca kriyābhir na tapobhir ugraiḥ / evaṃrūpaḥ śakya ahaṃ nṛloke; draṣṭuṃ tvadanyena kurupravīra /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 48: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mā te vyathā mā ca vimūḍhabhāvo; dṛṣṭvā rūpaṃ ghoram īdṛṅ mamedam / vyapetabhīḥ prītamanāḥ punas tvaṃ; tad eva me rūpam idaṃ prapaśya /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 49: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / ity arjunaṃ vāsudevas tathoktvā; svakaṃ rūpaṃ darśayām āsa bhūyaḥ / āśvāsayām āsa ca bhītam enaṃ; bhūtvā punaḥ saumyavapur mahātmā /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 50: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / dṛṣṭvedaṃ mānuṣaṃ rūpaṃ tava saumyaṃ janārdana / idānīm asmi saṃvṛttaḥ sacetāḥ prakṛtiṃ gataḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 51: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / sudurdarśam idaṃ rūpaṃ dṛṣṭavān asi yan mama / devā apy asya rūpasya nityaṃ darśanakāṅkṣiṇaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 52: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nāhaṃ vedair na tapasā na dānena na cejyayā / śakya evaṃvidho draṣṭuṃ dṛṣṭavān asi māṃ yathā /
verses.translation: In the Vishwarupa Darshana Yoga (Chapter 11), verse 53: Arjuna is granted divine vision and beholds Krishna's awesome, infinite cosmic form — the destroyer and sustainer of all worlds. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: bhaktyā tv ananyayā śakya aham evaṃvidho 'rjuna / jñātuṃ draṣṭuṃ ca tattvena praveṣṭuṃ ca paraṃtapa /
verses.translation: My dear Arjuna, only by undivided devotional service can I be understood as I am, standing before you, and can thus be seen directly. Only in this way can you enter into the mysteries of My understanding.

verses.transliteration: matkarmakṛn matparamo madbhaktaḥ saṅgavarjitaḥ / nirvairaḥ sarvabhūteṣu yaḥ sa mām eti pāṇḍava /
verses.translation: My dear Arjuna, he who engages in My pure devotional service, free from the contaminations of fruitive activities and mental speculation, he who works for Me, who makes Me the supreme goal of his life, and who is friendly to every living being — he certainly comes to Me.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / evaṃ satatayuktā ye bhaktās tvāṃ paryupāsate / ye cāpy akṣaram avyaktaṃ teṣāṃ ke yogavittamāḥ /
verses.translation: Arjuna inquired: Which are considered to be more perfect, those who are always properly engaged in Your devotional service, or those who worship the impersonal Brahman, the unmanifested?

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / mayy āveśya mano ye māṃ nityayuktā upāsate / śraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Those who fix their minds on My personal form and are always engaged in worshipping Me with great and transcendental faith are considered by Me to be most perfect.

verses.transliteration: ye tv akṣaram anirdeśyam avyaktaṃ paryupāsate / sarvatragam acintyaṃ ca kūṭastham acalaṃ dhruvam /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 3: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: saṃniyamyendriyagrāmaṃ sarvatra samabuddhayaḥ / te prāpnuvanti mām eva sarvabhūtahite ratāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 4: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kleśo 'dhikataras teṣām avyaktāsaktacetasām / avyaktā hi gatir duḥkhaṃ dehavadbhir avāpyate /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 5: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ye tu sarvāṇi karmāṇi mayi saṃnyasya matparāḥ / ananyenaiva yogena māṃ dhyāyanta upāsate /
verses.translation: But those who worship Me with devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — to them I carry what they lack and preserve what they have.

verses.transliteration: teṣām ahaṃ samuddhartā mṛtyusaṃsārasāgarāt / bhavāmi nacirāt pārtha mayy āveśitacetasām /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 7: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhiṃ niveśaya / nivasiṣyasi mayy eva ata ūrdhvaṃ na saṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: Just fix your mind upon Me, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and engage all your intelligence in Me. Thus you will live in Me always, without a doubt.

verses.transliteration: atha cittaṃ samādhātuṃ na śaknoṣi mayi sthiram / abhyāsayogena tato mām icchāptuṃ dhanaṃjaya /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 9: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: abhyāse 'py asamartho 'si matkarmaparamo bhava / madartham api karmāṇi kurvan siddhim avāpsyasi /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 10: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: athaitad apy aśakto 'si kartuṃ madyogam āśritaḥ / sarvakarmaphalatyāgaṃ tataḥ kuru yatātmavān /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 11: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śreyo hi jñānam abhyāsāj jñānād dhyānaṃ viśiṣyate / dhyānāt karmaphalatyāgas tyāgāc chāntir anantaram /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 12: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: adveṣṭā sarvabhūtānāṃ maitraḥ karuṇa eva ca / nirmamo nirahaṃkāraḥ samaduḥkhasukhaḥ kṣamī /
verses.translation: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities, who does not think himself a proprietor and is free from false ego, who is equal in both happiness and distress, who is tolerant, always satisfied, self-controlled, and engaged in devotional service with determination, his mind and intelligence fixed on Me — such a devotee of Mine is very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: saṃtuṣṭaḥ satataṃ yogī yatātmā dṛḍhaniścayaḥ / mayy arpitamanobuddhir yo madbhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ /
verses.translation: He who is satisfied with gain which comes of its own accord, who is free from duality and does not envy, who is steady in both success and failure, is never entangled, although performing actions.

verses.transliteration: yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate ca yaḥ / harṣāmarṣabhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa ca me priyaḥ /
verses.translation: He for whom no one is put into difficulty and who is not disturbed by anxiety, who is steady in happiness and distress, is very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: anapekṣaḥ śucir dakṣa udāsīno gatavyathaḥ / sarvārambhaparityāgī yo madbhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ /
verses.translation: My devotee who is not dependent on the ordinary course of activities, who is pure, expert, without cares, free from all pains, and not striving for some result, is very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: yo na hṛṣyati na dveṣṭi na śocati na kāṅkṣati / śubhāśubhaparityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ /
verses.translation: One who neither rejoices nor grieves, who neither laments nor desires, and who renounces both auspicious and inauspicious things — such a devotee is very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: samaḥ śatrau ca mitre ca tathā mānāpamānayoḥ / śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkheṣu samaḥ saṅgavivarjitaḥ /
verses.translation: One who is equal to friends and enemies, who is equipoised in honour and dishonour, heat and cold, happiness and distress, fame and infamy, who is always free from contaminating association, always silent and satisfied with anything, who doesn't care for any residence, who is fixed in knowledge and who is engaged in devotional service — such a person is very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: tulyanindāstutir maunī saṃtuṣṭo yena kena cit / aniketaḥ sthiramatir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Bhakti Yoga (Chapter 12), verse 19: Krishna describes the qualities of his dear devotees and declares that devotion is the highest and easiest path to him. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ye tu dharmyāmṛtam idaṃ yathoktaṃ paryupāsate / śraddadhānā matparamā bhaktās te 'tīva me priyāḥ /
verses.translation: Those who follow this imperishable path of devotional service and who completely engage themselves with faith, making Me the supreme goal, are very, very dear to Me.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / idaṃ śarīraṃ kaunteya kṣetram ity abhidhīyate / etad yo vetti taṃ prāhuḥ kṣetrajña iti tadvidaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 1: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata / kṣetrakṣetrajñayor jñānaṃ yat taj jñānaṃ mataṃ mama /
verses.translation: O son of Kunti, this body is called the field, and one who knows this body is called the knower of the field.

verses.transliteration: tat kṣetraṃ yac ca yādṛk ca yadvikāri yataś ca yat / sa ca yo yatprabhāvaś ca tat samāsena me śṛṇu /
verses.translation: O scion of the Bharata dynasty, I am also the knower in all bodies, and to understand this body and its knower is called knowledge. That is My opinion.

verses.transliteration: ṛṣibhir bahudhā gītaṃ chandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛthak / brahmasūtrapadaiś caiva hetumadbhir viniścitaiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 4: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mahābhūtāny ahaṃkāro buddhir avyaktam eva ca / indriyāṇi daśaikaṃ ca pañca cendriyagocarāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 5: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: icchā dveṣaḥ sukhaṃ duḥkhaṃ saṃghātaś cetanā dhṛtiḥ / etat kṣetraṃ samāsena savikāram udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 6: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: amānitvam adambhitvam ahiṃsā kṣāntir ārjavam / ācāryopāsanaṃ śaucaṃ sthairyam ātmavinigrahaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 7: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: indriyārtheṣu vairāgyam anahaṃkāra eva ca / janmamṛtyujarāvyādhiduḥkhadoṣānudarśanam /
verses.translation: Humility; pridelessness; nonviolence; tolerance; simplicity; approaching a bona fide spiritual master; cleanliness; steadiness; self-control.

verses.transliteration: asaktir anabhiṣvaṅgaḥ putradāragṛhādiṣu / nityaṃ ca samacittatvam iṣṭāniṣṭopapattiṣu /
verses.translation: Renunciation of the objects of sense gratification; absence of false ego; the perception of the evil of birth, death, old age, and disease.

verses.transliteration: mayi cānanyayogena bhaktir avyabhicāriṇī / viviktadeśasevitvam aratir janasaṃsadi /
verses.translation: Detachment; freedom from entanglement with children, wife, home, and the rest; even-mindedness amid pleasant and unpleasant events; constant and unalloyed devotion to Me.

verses.transliteration: adhyātmajñānanityatvaṃ tattvajñānārthadarśanam / etaj jñānam iti proktam ajñānaṃ yad ato 'nyathā /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 11: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jñeyaṃ yat tat pravakṣyāmi yaj jñātvāmṛtam aśnute / anādimat paraṃ brahma na sat tan nāsad ucyate /
verses.translation: I shall now explain the knowable, knowing which you will taste the eternal. Brahman, the spirit, beginningless and subordinate to Me, lies beyond the cause and effect of this material world.

verses.transliteration: sarvataḥ pāṇipādaṃ tat sarvato 'kṣiśiromukham / sarvataḥ śrutimal loke sarvam āvṛtya tiṣṭhati /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 13: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvendriyaguṇābhāsaṃ sarvendriyavivarjitam / asaktaṃ sarvabhṛc caiva nirguṇaṃ guṇabhoktṛ ca /
verses.translation: Everywhere are His hands and legs, His eyes, heads and faces, and He has ears everywhere. In this way the Supersoul exists, pervading everything.

verses.transliteration: bahir antaś ca bhūtānām acaraṃ caram eva ca / sūkṣmatvāt tad avijñeyaṃ dūrasthaṃ cāntike ca tat /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 15: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: avibhaktaṃ ca bhūteṣu vibhaktam iva ca sthitam / bhūtabhartṛ ca taj jñeyaṃ grasiṣṇu prabhaviṣṇu ca /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 16: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jyotiṣām api taj jyotis tamasaḥ param ucyate / jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ jñānagamyaṃ hṛdi sarvasya viṣṭhitam /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 17: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: iti kṣetraṃ tathā jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ coktaṃ samāsataḥ / madbhakta etad vijñāya madbhāvāyopapadyate /
verses.translation: Thus the field of activities (the body), knowledge, and the knowable have been described by Me. Only My devotees can understand this thoroughly and thus attain to My nature.

verses.transliteration: prakṛtiṃ puruṣaṃ caiva viddhy anādī ubhāv api / vikārāṃś ca guṇāṃś caiva viddhi prakṛtisaṃbhavān /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 19: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāryakāraṇakartṛtve hetuḥ prakṛtir ucyate / puruṣaḥ sukhaduḥkhānāṃ bhoktṛtve hetur ucyate /
verses.translation: Nature is said to be the cause of all material causes and effects, whereas the living entity is the cause of the various sufferings and enjoyments in this world.

verses.transliteration: puruṣaḥ prakṛtistho hi bhuṅkte prakṛtijān guṇān / kāraṇaṃ guṇasaṅgo 'sya sadasadyonijanmasu /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 21: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: upadraṣṭānumantā ca bhartā bhoktā maheśvaraḥ / paramātmeti cāpy ukto dehe 'smin puruṣaḥ paraḥ /
verses.translation: The living entity in material nature thus follows the ways of life, enjoying the three modes of nature. This is due to his association with that material nature. Thus he meets with good and evil among various species.

verses.transliteration: ya evaṃ vetti puruṣaṃ prakṛtiṃ ca guṇaiḥ saha / sarvathā vartamāno 'pi na sa bhūyo 'bhijāyate /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 23: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dhyānenātmani paśyanti ke cid ātmānam ātmanā / anye sāṃkhyena yogena karmayogena cāpare /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 24: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anye tv evam ajānantaḥ śrutvānyebhya upāsate / te 'pi cātitaranty eva mṛtyuṃ śrutiparāyaṇāḥ /
verses.translation: Some perceive the Supersoul within themselves through meditation, others through the cultivation of knowledge, and still others through working without fruitive desires.

verses.transliteration: yāvat saṃjāyate kiṃ cit sattvaṃ sthāvarajaṅgamam / kṣetrakṣetrajñasaṃyogāt tad viddhi bharatarṣabha /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 26: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: samaṃ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṃ parameśvaram / vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṃ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati /
verses.translation: Truly, the Supreme Lord is situated in the heart of all living beings. Those who see in all beings the Supersoul, the Supreme Lord, along with the mortal being — they actually see.

verses.transliteration: samaṃ paśyan hi sarvatra samavasthitam īśvaram / na hinasty ātmanātmānaṃ tato yāti parāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: One who sees the Supersoul everywhere and in every living being does not degrade himself by his mind. Thus he approaches the transcendental destination.

verses.transliteration: prakṛtyaiva ca karmāṇi kriyamāṇāni sarvaśaḥ / yaḥ paśyati tathātmānam akartāraṃ sa paśyati /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 29: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā bhūtapṛthagbhāvam ekastham anupaśyati / tata eva ca vistāraṃ brahma saṃpadyate tadā /
verses.translation: One who can see that all activities are performed by the body, which is created of material nature, and sees that the self does nothing, actually sees.

verses.transliteration: anāditvān nirguṇatvāt paramātmāyam avyayaḥ / śarīrastho 'pi kaunteya na karoti na lipyate /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 31: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathā sarvagataṃ saukṣmyād ākāśaṃ nopalipyate / sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 32: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yathā prakāśayaty ekaḥ kṛtsnaṃ lokam imaṃ raviḥ / kṣetraṃ kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṃ prakāśayati bhārata /
verses.translation: In the Kshetra Kshetrajna Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 13), verse 33: Krishna distinguishes between the field (body) and the knower of the field (soul), and teaches the twenty qualities of true knowledge. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kṣetrakṣetrajñayor evam antaraṃ jñānacakṣuṣā / bhūtaprakṛtimokṣaṃ ca ye vidur yānti te param /
verses.translation: O son of Bharata, as the sun alone illuminates all this universe, so does the living entity, one within the body, illuminate the entire body by consciousness.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / paraṃ bhūyaḥ pravakṣyāmi jñānānāṃ jñānam uttamam / yaj jñātvā munayaḥ sarve parāṃ siddhim ito gatāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 1: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: idaṃ jñānam upāśritya mama sādharmyam āgatāḥ / sarge 'pi nopajāyante pralaye na vyathanti ca /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 2: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṃ dadhāmy aham / saṃbhavaḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ tato bhavati bhārata /
verses.translation: The total material substance, called Brahman, is the source of birth, and it is that Brahman that I impregnate, making possible the births of all living beings, O son of Bharata.

verses.transliteration: sarvayoniṣu kaunteya mūrtayaḥ saṃbhavanti yāḥ / tāsāṃ brahma mahad yonir ahaṃ bījapradaḥ pitā /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 4: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ rajas tama iti guṇāḥ prakṛtisaṃbhavāḥ / nibadhnanti mahābāho dehe dehinam avyayam /
verses.translation: Material nature consists of three modes — goodness, passion, and ignorance. When the eternal living entity comes in contact with nature, O mighty-armed Arjuna, he becomes conditioned by these modes.

verses.transliteration: tatra sattvaṃ nirmalatvāt prakāśakam anāmayam / sukhasaṅgena badhnāti jñānasaṅgena cānagha /
verses.translation: O sinless one, the mode of goodness, being purer than the others, is illuminating, and it frees one from all sinful reactions. Those situated in that mode become conditioned by a sense of happiness and knowledge.

verses.transliteration: rajo rāgātmakaṃ viddhi tṛṣṇāsaṅgasamudbhavam / tan nibadhnāti kaunteya karmasaṅgena dehinam /
verses.translation: The mode of passion is born of unlimited desires and longings, O son of Kunti, and because of this the embodied living entity is bound to material fruitive actions.

verses.transliteration: tamas tv ajñānajaṃ viddhi mohanaṃ sarvadehinām / pramādālasyanidrābhis tan nibadhnāti bhārata /
verses.translation: O son of Bharata, know that the mode of darkness, born of ignorance, is the delusion of all embodied living entities. The results of this mode are madness, indolence, and sleep, which bind the conditioned soul.

verses.transliteration: sattvaṃ sukhe saṃjayati rajaḥ karmaṇi bhārata / jñānam āvṛtya tu tamaḥ pramāde saṃjayaty uta /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 9: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: rajas tamaś cābhibhūya sattvaṃ bhavati bhārata / rajaḥ sattvaṃ tamaś caiva tamaḥ sattvaṃ rajas tathā /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 10: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvadvāreṣu dehe 'smin prakāśa upajāyate / jñānaṃ yadā tadā vidyād vivṛddhaṃ sattvam ity uta /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 11: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: lobhaḥ pravṛttir ārambhaḥ karmaṇām aśamaḥ spṛhā / rajasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe bharatarṣabha /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 12: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aprakāśo 'pravṛttiś ca pramādo moha eva ca / tamasy etāni jāyante vivṛddhe kurunandana /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 13: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yadā sattve pravṛddhe tu pralayaṃ yāti dehabhṛt / tadottamavidāṃ lokān amalān pratipadyate /
verses.translation: When one dies in the mode of goodness, he attains the pure higher planets of the great sages.

verses.transliteration: rajasi pralayaṃ gatvā karmasaṅgiṣu jāyate / tathā pralīnas tamasi mūḍhayoniṣu jāyate /
verses.translation: When one dies in the mode of passion, he takes birth among those engaged in fruitive activities; and when one dies in the mode of ignorance, he takes birth in the animal kingdom.

verses.transliteration: karmaṇaḥ sukṛtasyāhuḥ sāttvikaṃ nirmalaṃ phalam / rajasas tu phalaṃ duḥkham ajñānaṃ tamasaḥ phalam /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 16: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sattvāt saṃjāyate jñānaṃ rajaso lobha eva ca / pramādamohau tamaso bhavato 'jñānam eva ca /
verses.translation: From the mode of goodness, real knowledge develops; from the mode of passion, greed develops; and from the mode of ignorance develop foolishness, madness, and illusion.

verses.transliteration: ūrdhvaṃ gacchanti sattvasthā madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ / jaghanyaguṇavṛttasthā adho gacchanti tāmasāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 18: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nānyaṃ guṇebhyaḥ kartāraṃ yadā draṣṭānupaśyati / guṇebhyaś ca paraṃ vetti madbhāvaṃ so 'dhigacchati /
verses.translation: When one properly sees that in all activities no other performer is at work than these modes of nature and he knows the Supreme Lord, who is transcendental to all these modes, he attains My spiritual nature.

verses.transliteration: guṇān etān atītya trīn dehī dehasamudbhavān / janmamṛtyujarāduḥkhair vimukto 'mṛtam aśnute /
verses.translation: When the embodied being is able to transcend these three modes associated with the material body, he can become free from birth, death, old age, and their distresses and can enjoy nectar even in this life.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / kair liṅgais trīn guṇān etān atīto bhavati prabho / kimācāraḥ kathaṃ caitāṃs trīn guṇān ativartate /
verses.translation: Arjuna inquired: O my dear Lord, by which symptoms is one known who is transcendental to these three modes? What is his behaviour? And how does he transcend the modes of nature?

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / prakāśaṃ ca pravṛttiṃ ca moham eva ca pāṇḍava / na dveṣṭi saṃpravṛttāni na nivṛttāni kāṅkṣati /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: O son of Pandu, he who does not hate illumination, attachment, and delusion when they are present or long for them when they disappear.

verses.transliteration: udāsīnavad āsīno guṇair yo na vicālyate / guṇā vartanta ity eva yo 'vatiṣṭhati neṅgate /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 23: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: samaduḥkhasukhaḥ svasthaḥ samaloṣṭāśmakāñcanaḥ / tulyapriyāpriyo dhīras tulyanindātmasaṃstutiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 24: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mānāpamānayos tulyas tulyo mitrāripakṣayoḥ / sarvārambhaparityāgī guṇātītaḥ sa ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Gunatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 14), verse 25: Krishna explains the three gunas (qualities of nature) — sattva, rajas, and tamas — how they bind the soul, and how to transcend them. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: māṃ ca yo 'vyabhicāreṇa bhaktiyogena sevate / sa guṇān samatītyaitān brahmabhūyāya kalpate /
verses.translation: One who engages in full devotional service, unfailing in all circumstances, at once transcends the modes of material nature and thus comes to the level of Brahman.

verses.transliteration: brahmaṇo hi pratiṣṭhāham amṛtasyāvyayasya ca / śāśvatasya ca dharmasya sukhasyaikāntikasya ca /
verses.translation: And I am the basis of the impersonal Brahman, which is immortal, imperishable, and eternal and is the constitutional position of ultimate happiness.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / ūrdhvamūlam adhaḥśākham aśvatthaṃ prāhur avyayam / chandāṃsi yasya parṇāni yas taṃ veda sa vedavit /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: It is said that there is an imperishable banyan tree that has its roots upward and its branches down and whose leaves are the Vedic hymns. One who knows this tree is the knower of the Vedas.

verses.transliteration: adhaś cordhvaṃ prasṛtās tasya śākhā; guṇapravṛddhā viṣayapravālāḥ / adhaś ca mūlāny anusaṃtatāni; karmānubandhīni manuṣyaloke /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 2: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na rūpam asyeha tathopalabhyate; nānto na cādir na ca saṃpratiṣṭhā / aśvattham enaṃ suvirūḍhamūlam; asaṅgaśastreṇa dṛḍhena chittvā /
verses.translation: The real form of this tree cannot be perceived in this world. No one can understand where it ends, where it begins, or where its foundation is. But with determination one must cut down this strongly rooted tree with the weapon of detachment.

verses.transliteration: tataḥ padaṃ tatparimārgitavyaṃ; yasmin gatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ / tam eva cādyaṃ puruṣaṃ prapadye; yataḥ pravṛttiḥ prasṛtā purāṇī /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 4: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: nirmānamohā jitasaṅgadoṣā; adhyātmanityā vinivṛttakāmāḥ / dvandvair vimuktāḥ sukhaduḥkhasaṃjñair; gacchanty amūḍhāḥ padam avyayaṃ tat /
verses.translation: Those who are free from false prestige, illusion, and false association, who understand the eternal, who are done with material lust, who are freed from the dualities of happiness and distress, and who, unbewildered, know how to surrender unto the Supreme Person attain to that eternal kingdom.

verses.transliteration: na tad bhāsayate sūryo na śaśāṅko na pāvakaḥ / yad gatvā na nivartante tad dhāma paramaṃ mama /
verses.translation: That supreme abode of Mine is not illumined by the sun or moon, nor by fire or electricity. Those who reach it never return to this material world.

verses.transliteration: mamaivāṃśo jīvaloke jīvabhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ / manaḥṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi prakṛtisthāni karṣati /
verses.translation: The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal fragmental parts. Due to conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind.

verses.transliteration: śarīraṃ yad avāpnoti yac cāpy utkrāmatīśvaraḥ / gṛhītvaitāni saṃyāti vāyur gandhān ivāśayāt /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 8: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrotraṃ cakṣuḥ sparśanaṃ ca rasanaṃ ghrāṇam eva ca / adhiṣṭhāya manaś cāyaṃ viṣayān upasevate /
verses.translation: The living entity, thus taking another gross body, obtains a certain type of ear, eye, tongue, nose, and sense of touch, which are grouped about the mind. He thus enjoys a particular set of sense objects.

verses.transliteration: utkrāmantaṃ sthitaṃ vāpi bhuñjānaṃ vā guṇānvitam / vimūḍhā nānupaśyanti paśyanti jñānacakṣuṣaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 10: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yatanto yoginaś cainaṃ paśyanty ātmany avasthitam / yatanto 'py akṛtātmāno nainaṃ paśyanty acetasaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 11: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yad ādityagataṃ tejo jagad bhāsayate 'khilam / yac candramasi yac cāgnau tat tejo viddhi māmakam /
verses.translation: The splendour of the sun, which dissipates the darkness of this whole world, comes from Me. And the splendour of the moon and the splendour of fire are also from Me.

verses.transliteration: gām āviśya ca bhūtāni dhārayāmy aham ojasā / puṣṇāmi cauṣadhīḥ sarvāḥ somo bhūtvā rasātmakaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 13: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃ vaiśvānaro bhūtvā prāṇināṃ deham āśritaḥ / prāṇāpānasamāyuktaḥ pacāmy annaṃ caturvidham /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 14: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvasya cāhaṃ hṛdi saṃniviṣṭo; mattaḥ smṛtir jñānam apohanaṃ ca / vedaiś ca sarvair aham eva vedyo; vedāntakṛd vedavid eva cāham /
verses.translation: I am seated in everyone's heart, and from Me come remembrance, knowledge, and forgetfulness. By all the Vedas, I am to be known. Indeed, I am the compiler of Vedanta, and I am the knower of the Vedas.

verses.transliteration: dvāv imau puruṣau loke kṣaraś cākṣara eva ca / kṣaraḥ sarvāṇi bhūtāni kūṭastho 'kṣara ucyate /
verses.translation: There are two classes of beings, the fallible and the infallible. In the material world every living entity is fallible, and in the spiritual world every living entity is called infallible.

verses.transliteration: uttamaḥ puruṣas tv anyaḥ paramātmety udāhṛtaḥ / yo lokatrayam āviśya bibharty avyaya īśvaraḥ /
verses.translation: In the Purushottama Yoga (Chapter 15), verse 17: The supreme secret: Krishna is the Purushottama (Supreme Person) who transcends both the perishable world and the imperishable soul. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yasmāt kṣaram atīto 'ham akṣarād api cottamaḥ / ato 'smi loke vede ca prathitaḥ puruṣottamaḥ /
verses.translation: Because I am transcendental, beyond both the fallible and the infallible, and because I am the greatest, I am celebrated both in the world and in the Vedas as that Supreme Person.

verses.transliteration: yo mām evam asaṃmūḍho jānāti puruṣottamam / sa sarvavid bhajati māṃ sarvabhāvena bhārata /
verses.translation: Whoever knows Me as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, without doubting, is the knower of everything. He therefore engages himself in full devotional service to Me, O son of Bharata.

verses.transliteration: iti guhyatamaṃ śāstram idam uktaṃ mayānagha / etad buddhvā buddhimān syāt kṛtakṛtyaś ca bhārata /
verses.translation: This is the most confidential part of the Vedic scriptures, O sinless one, and it is disclosed now by Me. Whoever understands this will become wise, and his endeavours will know perfection.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / abhayaṃ sattvasaṃśuddhir jñānayogavyavasthitiḥ / dānaṃ damaś ca yajñaś ca svādhyāyas tapa ārjavam /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Fearlessness; purification of one's existence; cultivation of spiritual knowledge; charity; self-control; performance of sacrifice; study of the Vedas; austerity; simplicity.

verses.transliteration: ahiṃsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śāntir apaiśunam / dayā bhūteṣv aloluptvaṃ mārdavaṃ hrīr acāpalam /
verses.translation: Nonviolence; truthfulness; freedom from anger; renunciation; tranquillity; aversion to faultfinding; compassion for all living entities; freedom from covetousness; gentleness; modesty; steady determination.

verses.transliteration: tejaḥ kṣamā dhṛtiḥ śaucam adroho nātimānitā / bhavanti saṃpadaṃ daivīm abhijātasya bhārata /
verses.translation: Vigour, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, absence of envy, and absence of the passion for honour — these transcendent qualities, O son of Bharata, belong to godly men endowed with divine nature.

verses.transliteration: dambho darpo 'timānaś ca krodhaḥ pāruṣyam eva ca / ajñānaṃ cābhijātasya pārtha saṃpadam āsurīm /
verses.translation: Pride, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance — these qualities belong to those of demonic nature, O son of Pritha.

verses.transliteration: daivī saṃpad vimokṣāya nibandhāyāsurī matā / mā śucaḥ saṃpadaṃ daivīm abhijāto 'si pāṇḍava /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 5: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dvau bhūtasargau loke 'smin daiva āsura eva ca / daivo vistaraśaḥ prokta āsuraṃ pārtha me śṛṇu /
verses.translation: O son of Pritha, in this world there are two kinds of created beings. One is called the divine and the other demonic. I have already explained to you at length the divine qualities. Now hear from Me of the demoniac.

verses.transliteration: pravṛttiṃ ca nivṛttiṃ ca janā na vidur āsurāḥ / na śaucaṃ nāpi cācāro na satyaṃ teṣu vidyate /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 7: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: asatyam apratiṣṭhaṃ te jagad āhur anīśvaram / aparasparasaṃbhūtaṃ kim anyat kāmahaitukam /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 8: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: etāṃ dṛṣṭim avaṣṭabhya naṣṭātmāno 'lpabuddhayaḥ / prabhavanty ugrakarmāṇaḥ kṣayāya jagato 'hitāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 9: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāmam āśritya duṣpūraṃ dambhamānamadānvitāḥ / mohād gṛhītvāsadgrāhān pravartante 'śucivratāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 10: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: cintām aparimeyāṃ ca pralayāntām upāśritāḥ / kāmopabhogaparamā etāvad iti niścitāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 11: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āśāpāśaśatair baddhāḥ kāmakrodhaparāyaṇāḥ / īhante kāmabhogārtham anyāyenārthasaṃcayān /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 12: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: idam adya mayā labdham idaṃ prāpsye manoratham / idam astīdam api me bhaviṣyati punar dhanam /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 13: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: asau mayā hataḥ śatrur haniṣye cāparān api / īśvaro 'ham ahaṃ bhogī siddho 'haṃ balavān sukhī /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 14: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āḍhyo 'bhijanavān asmi ko 'nyo 'sti sadṛśo mayā / yakṣye dāsyāmi modiṣya ity ajñānavimohitāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 15: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anekacittavibhrāntā mohajālasamāvṛtāḥ / prasaktāḥ kāmabhogeṣu patanti narake 'śucau /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 16: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ātmasaṃbhāvitāḥ stabdhā dhanamānamadānvitāḥ / yajante nāmayajñais te dambhenāvidhipūrvakam /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 17: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ ca saṃśritāḥ / mām ātmaparadeheṣu pradviṣanto 'bhyasūyakāḥ /
verses.translation: Bewildered by false ego, strength, pride, lust, and anger, the demon becomes envious of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is situated in his own body and in the bodies of others, and blasphemes against the real religion.

verses.transliteration: tān ahaṃ dviṣataḥ krūrān saṃsāreṣu narādhamān / kṣipāmy ajasram aśubhān āsurīṣv eva yoniṣu /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 19: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āsurīṃ yonim āpannā mūḍhā janmani janmani / mām aprāpyaiva kaunteya tato yānty adhamāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 20: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: trividhaṃ narakasyedaṃ dvāraṃ nāśanam ātmanaḥ / kāmaḥ krodhas tathā lobhas tasmād etat trayaṃ tyajet /
verses.translation: There are three gates leading to this hell — lust, anger, and greed. Every sane man should give these up, for they lead to the degradation of the soul.

verses.transliteration: etair vimuktaḥ kaunteya tamodvārais tribhir naraḥ / ācaraty ātmanaḥ śreyas tato yāti parāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 22: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yaḥ śāstravidhim utsṛjya vartate kāmakārataḥ / na sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṃ na parāṃ gatim /
verses.translation: In the Daivasura Sampad Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 16), verse 23: Krishna describes divine qualities (fearlessness, purity, compassion) and demonic qualities (pride, arrogance, cruelty). One must cultivate the divine. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tasmāc chāstraṃ pramāṇaṃ te kāryākāryavyavasthitau / jñātvā śāstravidhānoktaṃ karma kartum ihārhasi /
verses.translation: One should therefore understand what is duty and what is not duty by the regulations of the scriptures. Knowing such rules and regulations, one should act so that he may gradually be elevated.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / ye śāstravidhim utsṛjya yajante śraddhayānvitāḥ / teṣāṃ niṣṭhā tu kā kṛṣṇa sattvam āho rajas tamaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 1: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / trividhā bhavati śraddhā dehināṃ sā svabhāvajā / sāttvikī rājasī caiva tāmasī ceti tāṃ śṛṇu /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: According to the modes of nature acquired by the embodied soul, one's faith can be of three kinds — in goodness, in passion, or in ignorance. Now hear about this.

verses.transliteration: sattvānurūpā sarvasya śraddhā bhavati bhārata / śraddhāmayo 'yaṃ puruṣo yo yacchraddhaḥ sa eva saḥ /
verses.translation: O son of Bharata, according to one's existence under the various modes of nature, one evolves a particular kind of faith. The living being is said to be of a particular faith according to the modes he has acquired.

verses.transliteration: yajante sāttvikā devān yakṣarakṣāṃsi rājasāḥ / pretān bhūtagaṇāṃś cānye yajante tāmasā janāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 4: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aśāstravihitaṃ ghoraṃ tapyante ye tapo janāḥ / dambhāhaṃkārasaṃyuktāḥ kāmarāgabalānvitāḥ /
verses.translation: Those who undergo severe austerities and penances not recommended in the scriptures, performing them out of pride and egoism, who are impelled by lust and attachment, who are foolish and who torture the material elements of the body as well as the Supersoul dwelling within, are to be known as demons.

verses.transliteration: karśayantaḥ śarīrasthaṃ bhūtagrāmam acetasaḥ / māṃ caivāntaḥśarīrasthaṃ tān viddhy āsuraniścayān /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 6: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āhāras tv api sarvasya trividho bhavati priyaḥ / yajñas tapas tathā dānaṃ teṣāṃ bhedam imaṃ śṛṇu /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 7: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: āyuḥsattvabalārogyasukhaprītivivardhanāḥ / rasyāḥ snigdhāḥ sthirā hṛdyā āhārāḥ sāttvikapriyāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 8: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kaṭvamlalavaṇātyuṣṇatīkṣṇarūkṣavidāhinaḥ / āhārā rājasasyeṣṭā duḥkhaśokāmayapradāḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 9: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yātayāmaṃ gatarasaṃ pūti paryuṣitaṃ ca yat / ucchiṣṭam api cāmedhyaṃ bhojanaṃ tāmasapriyam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 10: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aphalākāṅkṣibhir yajño vidhidṛṣṭo ya ijyate / yaṣṭavyam eveti manaḥ samādhāya sa sāttvikaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 11: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: abhisaṃdhāya tu phalaṃ dambhārtham api caiva yat / ijyate bharataśreṣṭha taṃ yajñaṃ viddhi rājasam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 12: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: vidhihīnam asṛṣṭānnaṃ mantrahīnam adakṣiṇam / śraddhāvirahitaṃ yajñaṃ tāmasaṃ paricakṣate /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 13: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: devadvijaguruprājñapūjanaṃ śaucam ārjavam / brahmacaryam ahiṃsā ca śārīraṃ tapa ucyate /
verses.translation: Austerity of the body consists in worship of the Supreme Lord, the brahmanas, the spiritual master, and superiors like the father and mother, and in cleanliness, simplicity, celibacy, and nonviolence.

verses.transliteration: anudvegakaraṃ vākyaṃ satyaṃ priyahitaṃ ca yat / svādhyāyābhyasanaṃ caiva vāṅmayaṃ tapa ucyate /
verses.translation: Austerity of speech consists in speaking words that are truthful, pleasing, beneficial, and not agitating to others, and also in regularly reciting Vedic literature.

verses.transliteration: manaḥprasādaḥ saumyatvaṃ maunam ātmavinigrahaḥ / bhāvasaṃśuddhir ity etat tapo mānasam ucyate /
verses.translation: And satisfaction, simplicity, gravity, self-control, and purification of one's existence are the austerities of the mind.

verses.transliteration: śraddhayā parayā taptaṃ tapas tat trividhaṃ naraiḥ / aphalākāṅkṣibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṃ paricakṣate /
verses.translation: This threefold austerity, practised by men whose aim is not to benefit themselves materially but to please the Supreme, is of the nature of goodness.

verses.transliteration: satkāramānapūjārthaṃ tapo dambhena caiva yat / kriyate tad iha proktaṃ rājasaṃ calam adhruvam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 18: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: mūḍhagrāheṇātmano yat pīḍayā kriyate tapaḥ / parasyotsādanārthaṃ vā tat tāmasam udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 19: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dātavyam iti yad dānaṃ dīyate 'nupakāriṇe / deśe kāle ca pātre ca tad dānaṃ sāttvikaṃ smṛtam /
verses.translation: Charity given out of duty, without expectation of return, at the proper time and place, and to a worthy person is considered to be in the mode of goodness.

verses.transliteration: yat tu pratyupakārārthaṃ phalam uddiśya vā punaḥ / dīyate ca parikliṣṭaṃ tad dānaṃ rājasaṃ smṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 21: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: adeśakāle yad dānam apātrebhyaś ca dīyate / asatkṛtam avajñātaṃ tat tāmasam udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 22: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: oṃ tat sad iti nirdeśo brahmaṇas trividhaḥ smṛtaḥ / brāhmaṇās tena vedāś ca yajñāś ca vihitāḥ purā /
verses.translation: From the beginning of creation, the three words om tat sat were used to indicate the Supreme Absolute Truth. These three symbolic representations were used by brahmanas while chanting the hymns of the Vedas and during sacrifices for the satisfaction of the Supreme.

verses.transliteration: tasmād om ity udāhṛtya yajñadānatapaḥkriyāḥ / pravartante vidhānoktāḥ satataṃ brahmavādinām /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 24: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tad ity anabhisaṃdhāya phalaṃ yajñatapaḥkriyāḥ / dānakriyāś ca vividhāḥ kriyante mokṣakāṅkṣibhiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 25: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sadbhāve sādhubhāve ca sad ity etat prayujyate / praśaste karmaṇi tathā sacchabdaḥ pārtha yujyate /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 26: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yajñe tapasi dāne ca sthitiḥ sad iti cocyate / karma caiva tadarthīyaṃ sad ity evābhidhīyate /
verses.translation: In the Shraddhatraya Vibhaga Yoga (Chapter 17), verse 27: Krishna analyses the three types of faith, food, sacrifice, and austerity according to the three gunas — each reveals the spiritual nature of the practitioner. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: aśraddhayā hutaṃ dattaṃ tapas taptaṃ kṛtaṃ ca yat / asad ity ucyate pārtha na ca tat pretya no iha /
verses.translation: But sacrifices, austerities, and charities performed without faith in the Supreme are impermanent, O son of Pritha, regardless of whatever rites are performed. They are called asat and are useless both in this life and the next.

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / saṃnyāsasya mahābāho tattvam icchāmi veditum / tyāgasya ca hṛṣīkeśa pṛthak keśiniṣūdana /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: O mighty-armed one, I wish to understand the purpose of renunciation (tyaga) and of the renounced order of life (sannyasa), O killer of the Kesi demon, master of the senses.

verses.transliteration: śrībhagavān uvāca / kāmyānāṃ karmaṇāṃ nyāsaṃ saṃnyāsaṃ kavayo viduḥ / sarvakarmaphalatyāgaṃ prāhus tyāgaṃ vicakṣaṇāḥ /
verses.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: The giving up of activities that are based on material desire is what great learned men call the renounced order of life (sannyasa). And giving up the results of all activities is what the wise call renunciation (tyaga).

verses.transliteration: tyājyaṃ doṣavad ity eke karma prāhur manīṣiṇaḥ / yajñadānatapaḥkarma na tyājyam iti cāpare /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 3: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: niścayaṃ śṛṇu me tatra tyāge bharatasattama / tyāgo hi puruṣavyāghra trividhaḥ saṃprakīrtitaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 4: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yajñadānatapaḥkarma na tyājyaṃ kāryam eva tat / yajño dānaṃ tapaś caiva pāvanāni manīṣiṇām /
verses.translation: Acts of sacrifice, charity, and penance are not to be given up; they must be performed. Indeed, sacrifice, charity, and penance purify even the great souls.

verses.transliteration: etāny api tu karmāṇi saṅgaṃ tyaktvā phalāni ca / kartavyānīti me pārtha niścitaṃ matam uttamam /
verses.translation: All these activities should be performed without attachment or any expectation of result. They should be performed as a matter of duty, O son of Pritha. That is My final opinion.

verses.transliteration: niyatasya tu saṃnyāsaḥ karmaṇo nopapadyate / mohāt tasya parityāgas tāmasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 7: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: duḥkham ity eva yat karma kāyakleśabhayāt tyajet / sa kṛtvā rājasaṃ tyāgaṃ naiva tyāgaphalaṃ labhet /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 8: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kāryam ity eva yat karma niyataṃ kriyate 'rjuna / saṅgaṃ tyaktvā phalaṃ caiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ /
verses.translation: O Arjuna, when one performs his prescribed duty only because it ought to be done, and renounces all material association and all attachment to the fruit, his renunciation is said to be in the mode of goodness.

verses.transliteration: na dveṣṭy akuśalaṃ karma kuśale nānuṣajjate / tyāgī sattvasamāviṣṭo medhāvī chinnasaṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 10: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na hi dehabhṛtā śakyaṃ tyaktuṃ karmāṇy aśeṣataḥ / yas tu karmaphalatyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate /
verses.translation: It is indeed impossible for an embodied being to give up all activities. But he who renounces the fruits of action is called one who has truly renounced.

verses.transliteration: aniṣṭam iṣṭaṃ miśraṃ ca trividhaṃ karmaṇaḥ phalam / bhavaty atyāgināṃ pretya na tu saṃnyāsināṃ kva cit /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 12: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pañcaitāni mahābāho kāraṇāni nibodha me / sāṃkhye kṛtānte proktāni siddhaye sarvakarmaṇām /
verses.translation: O mighty-armed Arjuna, according to the Vedanta there are five causes for the accomplishment of all action. Now learn of these from Me.

verses.transliteration: adhiṣṭhānaṃ tathā kartā karaṇaṃ ca pṛthagvidham / vividhāś ca pṛthakceṣṭā daivaṃ caivātra pañcamam /
verses.translation: The place of action (the body), the performer, the various senses, the many different kinds of endeavour, and ultimately the Supersoul — these are the five factors of action.

verses.transliteration: śarīravāṅmanobhir yat karma prārabhate naraḥ / nyāyyaṃ vā viparītaṃ vā pañcaite tasya hetavaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 15: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: tatraivaṃ sati kartāram ātmānaṃ kevalaṃ tu yaḥ / paśyaty akṛtabuddhitvān na sa paśyati durmatiḥ /
verses.translation: Therefore one who thinks himself the only doer, not considering the five factors, is certainly not very intelligent and cannot see things as they are.

verses.transliteration: yasya nāhaṃkṛto bhāvo buddhir yasya na lipyate / hatvāpi sa imāṁl lokān na hanti na nibadhyate /
verses.translation: One who is not motivated by false ego, whose intelligence is not entangled, though he kills men in this world, does not kill. Nor is he bound by his actions.

verses.transliteration: jñānaṃ jñeyaṃ parijñātā trividhā karmacodanā / karaṇaṃ karma karteti trividhaḥ karmasaṃgrahaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 18: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: jñānaṃ karma ca kartā ca tridhaiva guṇabhedataḥ / procyate guṇasaṃkhyāne yathāvac chṛṇu tāny api /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 19: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sarvabhūteṣu yenaikaṃ bhāvam avyayam īkṣate / avibhaktaṃ vibhakteṣu taj jñānaṃ viddhi sāttvikam /
verses.translation: That knowledge by which one undivided spiritual nature is seen in all living entities, though they are divided into innumerable forms, you should understand to be in the mode of goodness.

verses.transliteration: pṛthaktvena tu yaj jñānaṃ nānābhāvān pṛthagvidhān / vetti sarveṣu bhūteṣu taj jñānaṃ viddhi rājasam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 21: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yat tu kṛtsnavad ekasmin kārye saktam ahaitukam / atattvārthavad alpaṃ ca tat tāmasam udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: But that knowledge by which one is attached to one kind of work as the all in all, without knowledge of the truth, and which is very meagre, is said to be in the mode of darkness.

verses.transliteration: niyataṃ saṅgarahitam arāgadveṣataḥ kṛtam / aphalaprepsunā karma yat tat sāttvikam ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 23: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yat tu kāmepsunā karma sāhaṃkāreṇa vā punaḥ / kriyate bahulāyāsaṃ tad rājasam udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 24: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: anubandhaṃ kṣayaṃ hiṃsām anapekṣya ca pauruṣam / mohād ārabhyate karma yat tat tāmasam ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 25: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: muktasaṅgo 'nahaṃvādī dhṛtyutsāhasamanvitaḥ / siddhyasiddhyor nirvikāraḥ kartā sāttvika ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 26: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: rāgī karmaphalaprepsur lubdho hiṃsātmako 'śuciḥ / harṣaśokānvitaḥ kartā rājasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 27: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ayuktaḥ prākṛtaḥ stabdhaḥ śaṭho naikṛtiko 'lasaḥ / viṣādī dīrghasūtrī ca kartā tāmasa ucyate /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 28: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: buddher bhedaṃ dhṛteś caiva guṇatas trividhaṃ śṛṇu / procyamānam aśeṣeṇa pṛthaktvena dhanaṃjaya /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 29: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: pravṛttiṃ ca nivṛttiṃ ca kāryākārye bhayābhaye / bandhaṃ mokṣaṃ ca yā vetti buddhiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī /
verses.translation: O son of Pritha, that understanding which knows what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, what is to be feared and what is not to be feared, what is binding and what is liberating, is in the mode of goodness.
