verses.transliteration: yayā dharmam adharmaṃ ca kāryaṃ cākāryam eva ca / ayathāvat prajānāti buddhiḥ sā pārtha rājasī /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 31: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: adharmaṃ dharmam iti yā manyate tamasāvṛtā / sarvārthān viparītāṃś ca buddhiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 32: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: dhṛtyā yayā dhārayate manaḥprāṇendriyakriyāḥ / yogenāvyabhicāriṇyā dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 33: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yayā tu dharmakāmārthān dhṛtyā dhārayate 'rjuna / prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣī dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha rājasī /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 34: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yayā svapnaṃ bhayaṃ śokaṃ viṣādaṃ madam eva ca / na vimuñcati durmedhā dhṛtiḥ sā pārtha tāmasī /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 35: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sukhaṃ tv idānīṃ trividhaṃ śṛṇu me bharatarṣabha / abhyāsād ramate yatra duḥkhāntaṃ ca nigacchati /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 36: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yat tadagre viṣam iva pariṇāme 'mṛtopamam / tat sukhaṃ sāttvikaṃ proktam ātmabuddhiprasādajam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 37: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: viṣayendriyasaṃyogād yat tadagre 'mṛtopamam / pariṇāme viṣam iva tat sukhaṃ rājasaṃ smṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 38: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: yad agre cānubandhe ca sukhaṃ mohanam ātmanaḥ / nidrālasyapramādotthaṃ tat tāmasam udāhṛtam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 39: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: na tad asti pṛthivyāṃ vā divi deveṣu vā punaḥ / sattvaṃ prakṛtijair muktaṃ yad ebhiḥ syāt tribhir guṇaiḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 40: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇakṣatriyaviśāṃ śūdrāṇāṃ ca paraṃtapa / karmāṇi pravibhaktāni svabhāvaprabhavair guṇaiḥ /
verses.translation: Brahmanas, kshatriyas, vaishyas, and shudras are distinguished by the qualities born of their own natures in accordance with the material modes, O chastiser of the enemy.

verses.transliteration: śamo damas tapaḥ śaucaṃ kṣāntir ārjavam eva ca / jñānaṃ vijñānam āstikyaṃ brahmakarma svabhāvajam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 42: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: śauryaṃ tejo dhṛtir dākṣyaṃ yuddhe cāpy apalāyanam / dānam īśvarabhāvaś ca kṣātraṃ karma svabhāvajam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 43: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: kṛṣigorakṣyavāṇijyaṃ vaiśyakarma svabhāvajam / paricaryātmakaṃ karma śūdrasyāpi svabhāvajam /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 44: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: sve sve karmaṇy abhirataḥ saṃsiddhiṃ labhate naraḥ / svakarmanirataḥ siddhiṃ yathā vindati tac chṛṇu /
verses.translation: By following his qualities of work, every man can become perfect. Now please hear from Me how this can be done.

verses.transliteration: yataḥ pravṛttir bhūtānāṃ yena sarvam idaṃ tatam / svakarmaṇā tam abhyarcya siddhiṃ vindati mānavaḥ /
verses.translation: By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all-pervading, a man can attain perfection through performing his own work.

verses.transliteration: śreyān svadharmo viguṇaḥ paradharmāt svanuṣṭhitāt / svabhāvaniyataṃ karma kurvan nāpnoti kilbiṣam /
verses.translation: It is better to engage in one's own occupation, even though one may perform it imperfectly, than to accept another's occupation and perform it perfectly. Duties prescribed according to one's nature are never affected by sinful reactions.

verses.transliteration: sahajaṃ karma kaunteya sadoṣam api na tyajet / sarvārambhā hi doṣeṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛtāḥ /
verses.translation: Every endeavour is covered by some fault, just as fire is covered by smoke. Therefore one should not give up the work born of his nature, O son of Kunti, even if such work is full of fault.

verses.transliteration: asaktabuddhiḥ sarvatra jitātmā vigataspṛhaḥ / naiṣkarmyasiddhiṃ paramāṃ saṃnyāsenādhigacchati /
verses.translation: One who is self-controlled and unattached and who disregards all material enjoyments can obtain, by practice of renunciation, the highest perfect stage of freedom from reaction.

verses.transliteration: siddhiṃ prāpto yathā brahma tathāpnoti nibodha me / samāsenaiva kaunteya niṣṭhā jñānasya yā parā /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 50: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto dhṛtyātmānaṃ niyamya ca / śabdādīn viṣayāṃs tyaktvā rāgadveṣau vyudasya ca /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 51: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: viviktasevī laghvāśī yatavākkāyamānasaḥ / dhyānayogaparo nityaṃ vairāgyaṃ samupāśritaḥ /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 52: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: ahaṃkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ parigraham / vimucya nirmamaḥ śānto brahmabhūyāya kalpate /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 53: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: brahmabhūtaḥ prasannātmā na śocati na kāṅkṣati / samaḥ sarveṣu bhūteṣu madbhaktiṃ labhate parām /
verses.translation: One who is thus transcendentally situated at once realises the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful. He never laments nor desires to have anything; he is equally disposed toward all living entities. In that state he attains pure devotional service unto Me.

verses.transliteration: bhaktyā mām abhijānāti yāvān yaś cāsmi tattvataḥ / tato māṃ tattvato jñātvā viśate tadanantaram /
verses.translation: One can understand Me as I am, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, only by devotional service. And when one is in full consciousness of Me by such devotion, he can enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.transliteration: sarvakarmāṇy api sadā kurvāṇo madvyapāśrayaḥ / matprasādād avāpnoti śāśvataṃ padam avyayam /
verses.translation: Though engaged in all kinds of activities, My pure devotee, under My protection, reaches the eternal and imperishable abode by My grace.

verses.transliteration: cetasā sarvakarmāṇi mayi saṃnyasya matparaḥ / buddhiyogam upāśritya maccittaḥ satataṃ bhava /
verses.translation: In all activities just depend upon Me and work always under My protection. In such devotional service, be fully conscious of Me.

verses.transliteration: maccittaḥ sarvadurgāṇi matprasādāt tariṣyasi / atha cet tvam ahaṃkārān na śroṣyasi vinaṅkṣyasi /
verses.translation: If you become conscious of Me, you will pass over all the obstacles of conditioned life by My grace. If, however, you do not work in such consciousness but act through false ego, not hearing Me, you will be lost.

verses.transliteration: yad ahaṃkāram āśritya na yotsya iti manyase / mithyaiṣa vyavasāyas te prakṛtis tvāṃ niyokṣyati /
verses.translation: In the Moksha Sanyasa Yoga (Chapter 18), verse 59: The conclusion: surrender completely to God. Krishna summarises all paths, declares his supreme instruction, and Arjuna resolves to fight with clarity. This verse is part of Krishna's teaching on this theme.

verses.transliteration: svabhāvajena kaunteya nibaddhaḥ svena karmaṇā / kartuṃ necchasi yan mohāt kariṣyasy avaśo 'pi tat /
verses.translation: Under illusion you are now declining to act according to My direction. But, compelled by the work born of your own nature, you will act all the same, O son of Kunti.

verses.transliteration: īśvaraḥ sarvabhūtānāṃ hṛddeśe 'rjuna tiṣṭhati / bhrāmayan sarvabhūtāni yantrārūḍhāni māyayā /
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord is situated in everyone's heart, O Arjuna, and is directing the wanderings of all living entities, who are seated as on a machine, made of the material energy.

verses.transliteration: tam eva śaraṇaṃ gaccha sarvabhāvena bhārata / tatprasādāt parāṃ śāntiṃ sthānaṃ prāpsyasi śāśvatam /
verses.translation: O descendant of Bharata, surrender unto Him utterly. By His grace you will attain transcendental peace and the supreme and eternal abode.

verses.transliteration: iti te jñānam ākhyātaṃ guhyād guhyataraṃ mayā / vimṛśyaitad aśeṣeṇa yathecchasi tathā kuru /
verses.translation: Thus I have explained to you knowledge still more confidential. Deliberate on this fully, and then do what you wish to do.

verses.transliteration: sarvaguhyatamaṃ bhūyaḥ śṛṇu me paramaṃ vacaḥ / iṣṭo 'si me dṛḍham iti tato vakṣyāmi te hitam /
verses.translation: Because you are My very dear friend, I am speaking to you My supreme instruction, the most confidential knowledge of all. Hear this from Me, for it is for your benefit.

verses.transliteration: manmanā bhava madbhakto madyājī māṃ namaskuru / mām evaiṣyasi satyaṃ te pratijāne priyo 'si me /
verses.translation: Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me, and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.

verses.transliteration: sarvadharmān parityajya mām ekaṃ śaraṇaṃ vraja / ahaṃ tvā sarvapāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ /
verses.translation: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.

verses.transliteration: idaṃ te nātapaskāya nābhaktāya kadā cana / na cāśuśrūṣave vācyaṃ na ca māṃ yo 'bhyasūyati /
verses.translation: This confidential knowledge may never be explained to those who are not austere, or devoted, or engaged in devotional service, nor to one who is envious of Me.

verses.transliteration: ya idaṃ paramaṃ guhyaṃ madbhakteṣv abhidhāsyati / bhaktiṃ mayi parāṃ kṛtvā mām evaiṣyaty asaṃśayaḥ /
verses.translation: For one who explains this supreme secret to the devotees, pure devotional service is guaranteed, and at the end he will come back to Me.

verses.transliteration: na ca tasmān manuṣyeṣu kaś cin me priyakṛttamaḥ / bhavitā na ca me tasmād anyaḥ priyataro bhuvi /
verses.translation: There is no servant in this world more dear to Me than he, nor will there ever be one more dear.

verses.transliteration: adhyeṣyate ca ya imaṃ dharmyaṃ saṃvādam āvayoḥ / jñānayajñena tenāham iṣṭaḥ syām iti me matiḥ /
verses.translation: And I declare that he who studies this sacred conversation of ours worships Me by his intelligence.

verses.transliteration: śraddhāvān anasūyaś ca śṛṇuyād api yo naraḥ / so 'pi muktaḥ śubhāṁl lokān prāpnuyāt puṇyakarmaṇām /
verses.translation: And one who listens with faith and without envy becomes free from sinful reactions and attains to the auspicious planets where the pious dwell.

verses.transliteration: kac cid etac chrutaṃ pārtha tvayaikāgreṇa cetasā / kac cid ajñānasaṃmohaḥ pranaṣṭas te dhanaṃjaya /
verses.translation: O conqueror of wealth, Arjuna, have you heard this with an attentive mind? And are your ignorance and illusions now dispelled?

verses.transliteration: arjuna uvāca / naṣṭo mohaḥ smṛtir labdhā tvatprasādān mayācyuta / sthito 'smi gatasaṃdehaḥ kariṣye vacanaṃ tava /
verses.translation: Arjuna said: My dear Krishna, O infallible one, my illusion is now gone. I have regained my memory by Your mercy. I am now firm and free from doubt and am prepared to act according to Your instructions.

verses.transliteration: saṃjaya uvāca / ity ahaṃ vāsudevasya pārthasya ca mahātmanaḥ / saṃvādam imam aśrauṣam adbhutaṃ romaharṣaṇam /
verses.translation: Sanjaya said: Thus I have heard the conversation of two great souls, Krishna and Arjuna. And so wonderful is that message that my hair is standing on end.

verses.transliteration: vyāsaprasādāc chrutavān etad guhyam ahaṃ param / yogaṃ yogeśvarāt kṛṣṇāt sākṣāt kathayataḥ svayam /
verses.translation: By the mercy of Vyasa, I have heard these most confidential talks directly from the master of all mysticism, Krishna, who was speaking personally to Arjuna.

verses.transliteration: rājan saṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya saṃvādam imam adbhutam / keśavārjunayoḥ puṇyaṃ hṛṣyāmi ca muhur muhuḥ /
verses.translation: O King, as I repeatedly recall this wondrous and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I take pleasure, being thrilled at every moment.

verses.transliteration: tac ca saṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya rūpam atyadbhutaṃ hareḥ / vismayo me mahān rājan hṛṣyāmi ca punaḥ punaḥ /
verses.translation: O King, as I remember the wonderful form of Lord Krishna, I am struck with wonder more and more, and I rejoice again and again.

verses.transliteration: yatra yogeśvaraḥ kṛṣṇo yatra pārtho dhanurdharaḥ / tatra śrīr vijayo bhūtir dhruvā nītir matir mama /
verses.translation: Wherever there is Krishna, the master of all mystics, and wherever there is Arjuna, the supreme archer, there will also certainly be opulence, victory, extraordinary power, and morality. That is my opinion.
